controls.title: Information Security Integration in Project Management
controls.objective: Ensure that information security is integrated into all project management processes, from initiation to closure, across all types of projects within the organization.
controls.successCriteria:
All projects demonstrate thorough integration of information security measures throughout their life cycle.
Documented evidence that information security risks are considered and treated as part of project management.
Clear definition and allocation of information security roles and responsibilities in all projects.
Compliance with organizational information security policies, regulations, and best practices in project management processes.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of current and recent projects managed by the organization.
Gather project management documentation for each project, including risk assessments, information security requirements, and meeting minutes from steering committee sessions.
Collect records of information security considerations and activities at different project stages.
Obtain documentation of roles and responsibilities related to information security within each project.

Analysis:
Review project documentation to ensure that information security risks are identified and treated early and throughout the project life cycle.
Verify that information security requirements are addressed in the early stages of projects.
Assess if the projects consider and treat information security risks associated with execution, such as secure communication.
Evaluate the periodic review process and effectiveness of the information security risk treatment within projects.
Confirm the appropriateness of information security considerations and activities against predefined stages and criteria.
Check if the responsibilities and authorities for information security are clearly defined and allocated within project roles.

Output:
A report on the integration of information security in project management for each reviewed project.
A compliance matrix indicating how each project aligns with the organization’s information security policies and procedures.
Recommendations for improvement in cases where projects fall short of the required information security integration standards.

controls.title: Anomalous Activity Monitoring Control
controls.objective: To ensure continuous monitoring of networks, systems, and applications for anomalous behavior, in alignment with business and information security requirements, and relevant laws and regulations.
controls.successCriteria:
Monitoring tools are configured appropriately and in line with organizational requirements.
All systems and applications are being effectively monitored for anomalies.
Established baseline of normal behavior is consistently applied, and deviations are accurately detected and alerted.
Response procedures to alerts are effective and timely.
No significant gaps in monitoring coverage of networks, systems, and applications.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve configuration details and settings of monitoring tools (e.g., IDS, IPS, firewalls).
Gather logs from monitoring tools for a defined period.
Obtain documentation outlining the baseline of normal behavior established for the organization.
Collect evidence of alerts generated by monitoring tools and responses to these alerts.
Secure a list of systems and applications under monitoring.

Analysis:
Review the monitoring tools' configurations against organizational requirements and best practices for detecting anomalous activities.
Analyze the collected logs to verify if monitoring is comprehensive (covering aspects like network traffic, access to critical systems, system and network configuration files, etc.).
Compare the monitoring against the established baseline of normal behavior to check for any deviations or anomalies.
Assess the effectiveness of alert generation and response mechanisms.
Cross-verify the list of monitored systems and applications with the organization’s inventory to ensure complete coverage.

Output:
Report detailing the configurations of monitoring tools.
Analysis report of the monitoring logs.
Documentation review report regarding the established baseline and response to alerts.
Compliance matrix indicating coverage of all systems and applications.

controls.title: Privileged Utility Program Management
controls.objective: To ensure the restricted and controlled use of utility programs capable of overriding system and application controls.
controls.successCriteria:
All utility programs are authorized, necessary, and used only by authorized personnel.
Authorization levels and access rights are properly defined, documented, and adhered to.
Ad hoc use of utility programs is adequately controlled and documented.
Effective segregation of utility programs is implemented and maintained.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all privileged utility programs used within the organization.
Acquire logs of user activities and access related to these utility programs.
Gather documented authorization levels and access rights for each utility program.
Collect records of ad hoc authorization requests for utility program use.
Retrieve evidence of segregation measures implemented for utility programs (e.g., network segregation documentation, application separation configurations).

Analysis:
Verify each privileged utility program against the list of authorized programs to confirm their necessity and authorized status.
Cross-reference user activity and access logs with authorized user lists to ensure only authorized personnel have used the utility programs.
Review the authorization levels and access rights to ensure they align with the documented standards and are properly implemented.
Examine ad hoc authorization requests for compliance with the policy and proper documentation.
Assess segregation measures to confirm logical and, where applicable, physical segregation of utility programs from application software and network traffic.

Output:
A report listing all privileged utility programs and their authorization status.
Spreadsheet or database entries correlating user activities with authorized access lists.
Compiled documentation of authorization levels, access rights, and ad hoc authorizations.
Analysis report on segregation measures and their effectiveness.

controls.title: Retention of Personal Information
controls.objective: To ensure the retention of personal information aligns with the entity's defined objectives related to privacy, including adherence to legal and regulatory requirements.
controls.successCriteria:
Personal information retention practices are in strict adherence to the entity’s defined objectives and policies.
All systems comply with documented retention schedules.
Any changes to retention practices have been properly documented and comply with the privacy objectives.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Compile a list of systems used for storing personal information.
Obtain data retention policies and schedules applicable to each system.
Gather change logs or records indicating any modifications to retention practices since the last audit.

Analysis:
Review the retention policies and schedules to confirm they align with the entity's privacy objectives.
Compare actual data retention practices in each system against the documented policies and schedules.
Examine change logs to validate that any alterations in retention practices have followed the prescribed process and comply with privacy objectives.

Output:
A comprehensive report documenting the retention practices and their compliance with the entity's privacy objectives.
A spreadsheet comparing each system's retention practices against the documented policies and schedules.
A record of any changes in retention practices and the review process followed for these changes.

controls.title: Third-Party Disclosure Consent Verification
controls.objective: To ensure that personal information is disclosed to third parties only with the explicit consent of data subjects.
controls.successCriteria:
All instances of personal information disclosure to third parties are accompanied by pre-obtained explicit consent from data subjects.
Consent forms or logs are complete, accurate, and correspond to the specific instances of disclosure.
Third-party agreements are consistent with the data subjects' consent provisions.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of instances where personal information was disclosed to third parties.
Collect documented consent forms or digital consent logs for these disclosures.
Acquire third-party agreements that specify the conditions of information sharing.

Analysis:
For each instance of disclosure, verify that explicit consent was obtained from the data subject prior to the disclosure.
Review the consent forms or logs to confirm they clearly relate to the specific disclosure instance and are dated prior to the disclosure.
Cross-reference third-party agreements to ensure they align with the consent provided by data subjects.

Output:
A report detailing each instance of personal information disclosure to third parties.
Verification checklist for consent forms or logs.
Analysis of third-party agreements' compliance with explicit consent.

controls.title: Access to Personal Information
controls.objective: To ensure that data subjects have the ability to access their stored personal information and receive copies upon request, in accordance with the entity’s privacy objectives.
controls.successCriteria:
Data subjects are able to access their personal information in a timely and secure manner.
The entity's processes for handling access requests are in compliance with privacy objectives and regulatory standards.
All denials of access are appropriately justified and communicated to the data subjects.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of systems used for storing personal data accessible to data subjects.
Gather samples of requests and responses for access to personal information.
Review the entity's procedures for responding to and processing access requests.

Analysis:
Assess the entity's process for authenticating the identity of data subjects making access requests.
Evaluate the timeliness and appropriateness of the entity's responses to access requests.
Verify that data subjects are informed of the reasons for any denial of access to their personal information.

Output:
A report summarizing the evaluation of the entity's response process to data subject access requests.
A compliance checklist verifying the handling of each access request in line with the entity's privacy objectives.
Documentation of instances where access was denied, including reasons and communication with the data subject.

controls.title: Response Team Structure Management
controls.objective: Ensure a well-defined and competent team structure for effective response to disruptions.
controls.successCriteria:
All response teams and their alternates are clearly identified in the organizational chart.
Roles and responsibilities of each team member are documented and align with control requirements.
Procedures guiding team actions are complete, clear, and cover necessary aspects.
Team members have undergone required training and possess the necessary competence.
Communication with relevant parties during disruptions follows documented procedures and is well-documented.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:

Obtain the organizational chart that identifies the response teams and their members.
Collect role descriptions and responsibilities for each team member and their alternates.
Gather documented procedures guiding the actions of each team.
Acquire training records and competency assessments for team members.
Retrieve communication logs showing interactions with relevant parties during disruptions.

Analysis:

Organizational Chart Review:
Verify that all response teams and their members are clearly identified.
Check for the presence of alternates for key roles.

Role and Responsibility Assessment:
Compare role descriptions with the responsibilities listed in section 8.4.2.
Ensure each team’s role and responsibilities align with the control requirements (8.4.2.2 and 8.4.2.4).

Procedure Documentation:
Review the documented procedures for completeness and clarity.
Confirm that procedures cover activation, operation, coordination, and communication (8.4.2.4b).

Competency and Training Verification:
Assess training records to ensure all team members and alternates have received appropriate training.
Verify competency assessments against the required skills and responsibilities.

Communication and Coordination Logs:
Examine logs to ensure communication with relevant parties, authorities, and media was timely and accurate.
Confirm that communication actions align with documented procedures.

Output:

Organizational Chart Analysis:
Annotated organizational chart with identified teams and roles.

Role and Responsibility Matrix:
Spreadsheet detailing roles, responsibilities, and their alignment with control requirements.

Procedure Review Summary:
Document summarizing the review of procedures and any identified gaps.

Training and Competency Report:
Spreadsheet listing team members, training dates, and competency assessments.

Communication Log Analysis:
Report analyzing communication logs and their compliance with documented procedures.

controls.title: Risk Treatment Plan Implementation
controls.objective: To ensure that risk responses are effectively identified, prioritized, and integrated into the Risk Treatment Plan, aligning with the organization's risk appetite and mitigation strategies.
controls.successCriteria:
The Risk Treatment Plan is up-to-date, comprehensive, and aligns with the organization's risk appetite.
All risk response actions are in alignment with the stated strategies in the Risk Treatment Plan.
Risk prioritization decisions are documented, justified, and align with the organization's strategic objectives and risk management framework.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the latest Risk Treatment Plan document.
Gather documentation of risk assessments and risk response actions taken since the last audit.
Collect records of risk review meetings and decision-making processes related to risk prioritization.

Analysis:
Review the Risk Treatment Plan to ensure it includes strategies for eliminating, mitigating, transferring, or accepting each identified risk.
For each risk response action taken, compare it against the Risk Treatment Plan to validate alignment and effectiveness.
Assess the decision-making process for prioritizing risks, ensuring it aligns with the organization's risk appetite and mitigation strategies.

Output:
A detailed report on the alignment of risk response actions with the Risk Treatment Plan.
A compliance matrix comparing each identified risk with its corresponding response action.
Documentation summarizing the effectiveness and appropriateness of the risk prioritization process.

controls.title: Secure Area Access and Activity Control
controls.objective: To ensure that access to and activities within secure areas are managed effectively, maintaining confidentiality, integrity, and availability of assets and information.
controls.successCriteria:
All access to secure areas is authorized and monitored.
No unauthorized or unsupervised activities are occurring within secure areas.
Recording and endpoint devices are used in compliance with organizational policies.
Emergency procedures are appropriately posted and accessible in all secure areas.
Information about secure areas is only disclosed on a need-to-know basis.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a current list of all secure areas within the organization.
Collect recent access logs and surveillance footage (if available) of these secure areas.
Gather policies and procedures related to secure area access and equipment usage.
Acquire records of any authorization forms for recording equipment or endpoint devices in secure areas.
Get copies of posted emergency procedures in each secure area.

Analysis:
Review access logs to verify that access is limited to authorized personnel.
Compare surveillance footage with access logs to check for unsupervised work or unauthorized access.
Assess adherence to policies regarding the use of recording equipment and endpoint devices within secure areas.
Validate that emergency procedures are properly posted and accessible in all secure areas.
Ensure that awareness of the existence of or activities within secure areas is restricted to individuals with a need-to-know basis.

Output:
A report summarizing findings from access log and surveillance footage analysis.
A compliance assessment report regarding the use of recording and endpoint devices.
An evaluation of the visibility and accessibility of emergency procedures in secure areas.

controls.title: Secure Development and Testing Control
controls.objective: To ensure that all new information systems, upgrades, and new versions undergo thorough security testing and verification during the development and acceptance processes, in line with established security requirements and best practices.
controls.successCriteria:
All reviewed projects demonstrate comprehensive security testing in accordance with the secure development policy.
Security testing activities cover all critical areas: security functions, secure coding, and secure configurations.
Effective management and security assessment of third-party components are evident.
The existence and effectiveness of WAF implementations and distinct environments for development, testing, and production are confirmed.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the development and deployment documentation for recent projects, including security testing plans and reports.
Gather evidence of security testing activities, such as results from static and dynamic analysis, code reviews, penetration test reports, and security assessments of third-party components.
Collect documentation on the separation of development, testing, and production environments.

Analysis:
Review the security testing documentation to ensure comprehensive coverage of security functions, secure coding, and secure configurations.
Evaluate the adherence to secure development lifecycle processes, including threat modeling, risk-based security requirements, and security testing methodologies.
Assess the effectiveness of the Web Application Firewall (WAF) implementations through its configuration and update history.
Confirm the proper management and security assessment of third-party components.
Verify the implementation of distinct environments for development, testing, and production.

Output:
A report detailing the findings of the security testing documentation review.
A compliance matrix aligning the actual security testing activities with the organization's secure development policy.
An assessment report on the effectiveness of the WAF and third-party component management.

controls.title: Engagement with Special Interest Groups
controls.objective: To ensure the organization actively participates in and benefits from engagement with relevant special interest groups and security forums for information security improvement and awareness.
controls.successCriteria:
Active and relevant participation in identified special interest groups and forums.
Regular and effective internal dissemination of information and best practices acquired from these groups.
Demonstrable impact of the information acquired on the organization's information security posture and practices.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of special interest groups and security forums in which the organization is a member.
Gather documentation or electronic records of interactions, such as meeting minutes, emails, and information bulletins received from these groups.
Collect records of internal dissemination of information acquired from these groups, such as emails, newsletters, or presentations to staff.

Analysis:
Verify the relevance and activity level of each group or forum to the organization's information security needs.
Review the documentation or records of interactions to assess the regularity and quality of information exchange.
Assess how the information from these groups is utilized internally to improve information security practices.

Output:
A report detailing the organization's engagement with each group or forum.
An assessment report on the effectiveness of information dissemination within the organization.
Recommendations for improving engagement and information utilization.

controls.title: Cloud Service Management
controls.objective: To ensure the acquisition, use, management, and exit from cloud services are conducted in alignment with the organization's information security policies and standards.
controls.successCriteria:
All cloud services are in alignment with the organization’s selection criteria and usage policies.
Cloud service acquisition and exit processes comply with the organization's policies and procedures.
Effective management of information security risks associated with cloud services.
Proper handling and response to security incidents in cloud services as per the incident management policy.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all active cloud services and contracts used by the organization.
Gather documentation on cloud service acquisition and exit processes.
Collect evidence of cloud service management activities, including security reviews, risk assessments, and compliance checks.
Acquire incident management logs related to cloud services.

Analysis:
Review the list of cloud services against approved cloud service selection criteria and scope of usage.
Assess the adequacy of cloud service acquisition and exit processes against established policies and procedures.
Evaluate the effectiveness of cloud service management activities, focusing on how information security risks are identified, managed, and mitigated.
Analyze incident management logs to ensure proper handling and response to security incidents in cloud environments.

Output:
A report detailing each cloud service and its compliance status with selection criteria and usage scope.
A compliance assessment report for cloud service acquisition and exit processes.
An evaluation report of cloud service management activities.
An analysis report of incident management logs related to cloud services.

controls.title: Physical Security Perimeter Audit
controls.objective: To ensure that the physical security measures in place effectively protect the organization's facilities, assets, and proprietary information against unauthorized access or physical threats.
controls.successCriteria:
All physical security perimeters are in good condition and provide adequate protection.
All surveillance and access control systems are functioning as intended, with no unauthorized access or breaches identified.
Regular maintenance and testing of physical security systems are being conducted in compliance with the policy.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a comprehensive list of all physical security perimeters (e.g., fences, walls, doors, barriers) around the organization's facilities.
Collect records of recent physical security tests and maintenance activities.
Gather footage or logs from surveillance systems (like CCTV) and access control systems for a specified period.

Analysis:
Evaluate the condition and effectiveness of each physical perimeter, checking for any vulnerabilities or breaches.
Review the maintenance and testing records to confirm regular upkeep and functionality of physical security barriers.
Analyze surveillance and access control logs to identify any unauthorized access attempts or security breaches.

Output:
A detailed report on the state and effectiveness of each security perimeter.
A log analysis report highlighting any security incidents or unusual activities.
A maintenance and testing compliance report for physical security systems.

controls.title: Information and Asset Use Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that all individuals accessing or using the organization's information and associated assets adhere to the defined standards of acceptable use, maintaining the integrity, confidentiality, and security of these assets.
controls.successCriteria:
All users comply with the acceptable use standards as per the Acceptable Use Policy.
Effective monitoring mechanisms are in place to detect and report non-compliant activities.
Incidents of policy violations are minimal and appropriately addressed.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the current Inventory of Information and Associated Assets document.
Gather comprehensive details for each asset, including network addresses (if static), hardware addresses, machine names, data asset owner, and department.
Acquire evidence of network connection approvals for each asset, particularly for mobile end-user devices.
Gather ownership assignment records for each listed asset and signed asset return forms for terminated or changed employment cases.
Acquire logs of recent updates or changes made to the asset inventory and records of asset tracking upon employee exit.

Analysis:
Review the inventory document for completeness, accuracy, and inclusion of additional required details such as network and hardware addresses, machine names, data asset owners, departments, and network approval status.
Cross-check ownership records against each asset and returned assets against the Asset Registry.
Analyze change logs and asset tracking logs to verify that inventory updates, including the additional details, and asset returns are consistent, timely, and comply with policy.

Output:
A comprehensive report detailing findings from the inventory review, including the verification of additional asset details.
A spreadsheet or database capturing the alignment of ownership records with the enhanced asset inventory and returned assets.
Summary of discrepancies or gaps identified during the analysis, especially in regards to the newly added asset details.

controls.title: Script Allowlisting Control
controls.objective: Ensure that only authorized scripts, such as specific .ps1, .py, etc., files, are allowed to execute on company systems. Unauthorized scripts must be blocked to maintain system integrity and security.
controls.successCriteria:
All script executions match the authorized scripts on the allowlist, with valid digital signatures and correct version control.
No evidence of unauthorized script executions on the systems.
System configurations effectively block unauthorized scripts.
All changes to the script allowlist have been properly authorized, documented, and aligned with the IT Security Policy.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Get the list of IT systems where script execution is relevant.
Retrieve the allowlist of authorized scripts, including details like script names, digital signatures, and version numbers.
Collect system logs showing script execution attempts and outcomes.
Obtain change management records for updates or modifications to the script allowlist.

Analysis:
For each script execution logged, verify against the allowlist to ensure only authorized scripts have been executed.
Examine the digital signatures and version numbers of the executed scripts to ensure they match the authorized versions.
Review system configurations to confirm that controls for blocking unauthorized scripts are operational.
Analyze change management records to validate that updates to the script allowlist have been properly authorized and documented.

Output:
List of all IT systems reviewed.
Spreadsheet or report detailing each script execution against the allowlist.
System configuration snapshots demonstrating script blocking controls.
Compiled change management records related to the script allowlist.

controls.title: Environment Segregation Control
controls.objective: To ensure that development, testing, and production environments are adequately separated and secured to prevent unauthorized access and changes, and to mitigate the risk of production system disruptions.
controls.successCriteria:
Clear and documented segregation between development, testing, and production environments.
Access controls and change management procedures are in compliance with the segregation policy.
No unauthorized access or changes in the production environment.
Sensitive information in development and testing environments is adequately protected.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all systems and environments categorized as development, testing, and production.
Gather configuration details and access control lists for each environment.
Collect recent change management logs and deployment records from development to production.

Analysis:
Verify that each environment (development, testing, production) is operating in separate domains or physical/virtual environments.
Check that access controls are in place and align with the segregation policy, ensuring that compilers, editors, and development tools are not accessible from production systems unless necessary.
Review change management logs to confirm that changes to production systems are tested in the testing or staging environment first and that there are no unauthorized changes or tests in the production environment.
Assess if sensitive information is copied into the development and testing system environments and if equivalent controls are in place.

Output:
A report detailing the segregation and security measures of each environment.
A spreadsheet analyzing access controls and compliance with the segregation policy.
A change management review report.

controls.title: Data Subject Information Request Management
controls.objective: To efficiently manage and respond to data subjects' requests for an accounting of their personal information and disclosures.
controls.successCriteria:
All requests for information are responded to in a timely and accurate manner as per policy.
Responses to data subjects are complete and comply with the organization’s privacy objectives.
The process for handling information requests demonstrates adherence to legal and regulatory requirements.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Collect records of data subject information requests received since the last audit.
Obtain documentation of responses provided to these requests.
Review policies and procedures for managing and responding to data subject requests.

Analysis:
Assess each request and corresponding response to ensure compliance with policies and procedures.
Verify the completeness and accuracy of the information provided to data subjects.
Evaluate the timeliness of responses against policy standards.

Output:
A detailed report on data subject request handling.
Analysis summary of the adequacy and timeliness of responses.
Documentation of any discrepancies or non-compliance in handling requests.

controls.title: Information Security Policy Compliance
controls.objective:
To ensure that the organization's Information Security Policy 

and its topic-specific policies are up-to-date, approved by top management, communicated effectively, and reviewed regularly to reflect changes in business strategy, regulations, information security risks, and the threat environment.
controls.successCriteria:
All Information Security and topic-specific policies are current, reflect organizational needs, and are approved by top management.
Evidence of effective communication and acknowledgment of these policies by relevant personnel.
Regular reviews and updates of policies following changes in business strategy, regulatory environment, and information security landscape.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the latest version of the Information Security Policy and all topic-specific policies (e.g., access control, incident management, asset management).
Gather records of policy reviews, updates, and approvals by top management.
Collect evidence of policy communication and acknowledgment by relevant personnel and interested parties.
Acquire documentation of the latest management reviews and information security audits.

Analysis:
Review the Information Security Policy and topic-specific policies to ensure they are aligned with business strategy, legal requirements, risks, and threats.
Verify that policies have been approved by top management and that any changes are also approved.
Check that the policies have been communicated to and acknowledged by the relevant personnel.
Analyze the results of management reviews and audits to ascertain if they have led to any updates in policies.

Output:
A report verifying the current status and alignment of the Information Security Policy and topic-specific policies.
A compliance checklist for each policy, indicating approval, communication, and acknowledgment status.
Summary of findings from the review of management audits and their impact on policy updates.

controls.title: Network Services Security
controls.objective: Ensure the security mechanisms, service levels, and service requirements of network services are identified, implemented, and regularly monitored.
controls.successCriteria:
All network services meet the identified security measures and service requirements.
Monitoring of network services is regular and in compliance with the established procedures.
Third-party attestations are valid, current, and in line with organizational security standards.
Network and network service usage rules are comprehensive, up-to-date, and effectively enforced.
Network service usage logs align with established authentication and authorization procedures.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a comprehensive list of all network services used (both internal and external).
Gather documentation or evidence of security measures implementation for each network service (e.g., security features, service levels).
Acquire records of regular monitoring activities and audit rights exercised on network service providers.
Collect third-party attestations from network service providers, if applicable.
Retrieve current rules and policies related to network and network service usage.
Access records or logs of network service usage, including authentication and authorization details.

Analysis:
Cross-reference each network service against its corresponding security measures to ensure compliance.
Verify the regularity and thoroughness of monitoring activities.
Check for the existence and validity of third-party attestations.
Review network usage rules and policies for completeness and relevance.
Analyze network service usage logs for adherence to defined authentication and authorization procedures.

Output:
A report detailing the security measures in place for each network service.
A compliance matrix showing the alignment of each service with its respective security requirements.
Documentation or records of monitoring activities and third-party attestations.
A summary of findings on the review of network usage rules and policies.
An analysis report of network service usage logs.

controls.title: Correction and Amendment of Personal Information
controls.objective: To ensure the entity efficiently processes data subjects' requests to correct, amend, or append their personal information, and communicates these changes to relevant third parties.
controls.successCriteria:
Efficient and timely processing of data subjects' requests for correction, amendment, or appending of their personal information.
Compliance with established procedures for processing these requests and communicating changes to third parties.
Adequate documentation and tracking of correction/amendment requests and the entity's responses.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Compile a list of systems where personal data subject to correction/amendment requests is stored.
Gather samples of correction/amendment requests and the entity's responses.
Review the entity's procedures for processing and communicating corrections or amendments of personal information.

Analysis:
Assess the process for handling requests for correction or amendment, ensuring it aligns with the entity's privacy objectives.
Evaluate the timeliness and effectiveness of the entity's responses to such requests.
Verify that the entity communicates any corrections or amendments of personal information to third parties who have received this data.

Output:
A report summarizing the assessment of the entity's processes for handling and responding to correction/amendment requests.
A compliance checklist evaluating each request against the entity's procedural standards.
Documentation of instances where corrections/amendments were communicated to third parties.

controls.title: Physical Entry Security Control
controls.objective: To ensure secure areas are protected by appropriate entry controls and access points, mitigating the risk of unauthorized physical access.
controls.successCriteria:
All physical access control policies and procedures are up-to-date, comprehensive, and in line with industry best practices.
No instances of unauthorized access are detected in the access logs.
All physical access control systems are operational and properly maintained.
Visitor management is consistent with policy requirements, with all visitors authorized and logged appropriately.
Inspection procedures for personal belongings at entry and exit points comply with legal requirements and are effectively enforced.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve the current access control policy documentation and any related procedures.
Collect records of access logs from all entry points (e.g., main entrances, delivery and loading areas) for a specified period.
Gather details of physical access control systems in use (e.g., biometric, card readers, PIN systems).
Obtain visitor logs, including visitor identities, entry and exit times, and areas accessed.
Acquire the list and procedures of personal belongings inspections at entry and exit points.

Analysis:
Review access control policies and procedures to ensure they are comprehensive and in line with best practices.
Analyze access logs to identify any unauthorized access or anomalies (e.g., access during odd hours, access by unauthorized personnel).
Examine physical access control systems' configurations and maintenance records to ensure they are functioning as intended.
Cross-check visitor logs against authorized visitor lists to verify all visitors were authorized and appropriately supervised.
Assess the inspection procedures of personal belongings to ensure they comply with local laws and are effectively implemented.

Output:
A report detailing the findings of the policy and procedure review.
A spreadsheet or database of access log analysis highlighting any discrepancies or unauthorized access incidents.
A summary of physical access control systems' status and maintenance checks.
A report on visitor log analysis, indicating compliance with visitor management procedures.
An evaluation report on personal belongings inspection procedures.

controls.title: Secure Development Lifecycle Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure that all software and system development adhere to the organization’s Secure Development Life Cycle (SDLC) policy and guidelines.
controls.successCriteria:
All projects strictly follow the established SDLC steps and guidelines.
Secure coding practices are in line with organizational guidelines.
Third-party components are used in compliance with security assessments and updates.
Developers are adequately trained and demonstrate capability in secure development practices.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all ongoing and recently completed software and system development projects.
Collect SDLC documentation for each project, including threat models, security requirements, test results, code reviews, and penetration test reports.
Gather secure coding guideline documents and training records for developers.
Retrieve documentation regarding the use of third-party components and their security assessments.

Analysis:
For each project, verify that all stages of the SDLC were followed according to the policy.
Compare secure coding practices in the projects against the organization's guidelines.
Assess the use of third-party components, ensuring they have been cleared by the security team and are regularly updated.
Evaluate the training records to confirm developers’ proficiency in secure development practices.

Output:
A detailed report of SDLC adherence for each project.
A comparison table matching secure coding practices with organizational guidelines.
A summary of third-party component usage and compliance.
A compliance report on developer training and capabilities.

controls.title: Data Storage Integrity
controls.objective: Ensure the integrity and security of stored inputs, in-process items, and outputs in line with system specifications.
controls.successCriteria:
Storage activities for inputs, in-process items, and outputs align with defined specifications.
Effective protection measures are in place for stored data.
Adherence to archiving and data protection protocols.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve system storage policies and specifications.
Collect records and logs of storage activities for inputs, in-process items, and outputs.
Obtain documentation of data protection measures for stored items.

Analysis:
Compare storage policies with actual storage records to ensure compliance with completeness, accuracy, and timeliness requirements.
Assess data protection measures for effectiveness in preventing theft, corruption, or loss.
Review records for adherence to archiving and protection protocols.

Output:
Compliance report comparing storage activities with policy specifications.
Evaluation of data protection measures.
Summary of adherence to archiving and storage protection standards.

controls.title: POI Device Inspection Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that the frequency and type of POI device inspections are in accordance with the organization's targeted risk analysis and comply with relevant industry standards.
controls.successCriteria:
The targeted risk analysis for POI devices accurately reflects the required frequency and types of inspections and adheres to industry standards.
Documented results of POI device inspections are consistent with the targeted risk analysis.
Personnel demonstrate adequate understanding and implementation of POI device inspection procedures.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the organization's targeted risk analysis document that specifies the frequency and type of POI device inspections.
Gather documented results of recent POI device inspections.
Collect interviews or statements from personnel involved in POI device inspections.

Analysis:
Review the targeted risk analysis to confirm that it includes all elements specified for POI device inspections and is in line with industry standards.
Compare the documented inspection results with the requirements stated in the targeted risk analysis to verify consistency.
Assess personnel statements to ensure understanding and correct implementation of the inspection procedures.

Output:
A report summarizing the findings of the targeted risk analysis review.
A comparison spreadsheet or document detailing the alignment between the POI device inspection records and the targeted risk analysis.
Summary of personnel interviews or statements with insights on the practical execution of the inspection process.

controls.title: Personal Information Collection Control
controls.objective: To ensure that personal information is collected in a manner consistent with the entity's privacy objectives, focusing on necessity, fairness, and lawfulness.
controls.successCriteria:
Personal information collection practices align with the entity's privacy objectives.
Data is collected in a fair, lawful, and minimal manner, consistent with privacy standards and regulations.
Documented evidence of compliance with privacy policies and regulations in all data collection processes.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Collect a list of all systems and processes where personal information is gathered.
Obtain samples of data collection forms and methods used across these systems.
Review policies and procedures related to the collection of personal information.

Analysis:
Evaluate the data collection forms and methods to ensure they align with the entity's stated objectives for privacy.
Assess whether the collection of personal information is limited to what is necessary for the intended purposes.
Verify that the collection methods are fair and lawful, adhering to relevant privacy regulations and standards.

Output:
A detailed report on the evaluation of personal information collection practices.
A compliance matrix assessing each data collection method against privacy objectives and regulatory requirements.
Documentation of policy and procedure reviews pertaining to personal information collection.

controls.title: Secure Facility Management
controls.objective: Ensure effective physical security measures are in place to protect offices, rooms, and facilities from unauthorized access and threats.
controls.successCriteria:
The physical security plan comprehensively addresses all identified risks and complies with internal standards.
No unauthorized access or security breaches are evident in the logs and footage.
Recent audits and tests show continuous improvement and alignment with emerging threats.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the current physical security plan detailing measures for each site (including perimeter security, access control systems, CCTV placement, and emergency response plans).
Collect recent security logs from access control systems and CCTV footage.
Retrieve records of recent security audits, tests, and revisions to physical security measures.

Analysis:
Assess the physical security plan for adequacy in addressing identified risks and compliance with internal standards.
Analyze access control logs and CCTV footage for any unauthorized access attempts or security breaches.
Review the outcomes of recent security audits and tests to identify any gaps or areas for improvement.

Output:
A report on the effectiveness of the physical security plan.
A log analysis report highlighting any security incidents.
A summary of findings from the review of recent security audits and tests.
Audit Success Criteria:

controls.title: Clear Desk and Screen Compliance Check
controls.objective: To ensure adherence to the organization's clear desk and clear screen policy, thereby protecting sensitive information and maintaining data privacy.
controls.successCriteria:
Workspaces comply with the clear desk policy (no sensitive information left unattended, secure endpoint devices).
All printers comply with the authentication requirement of the clear screen policy.
System configurations adhere to the automatic logout and screen lock requirements.
Effective dissemination and understanding of the clear desk and clear screen policy across the organization.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence Collection:
Obtain the organization's clear desk and clear screen policy document.
Conduct surprise checks to take photographs of workspaces, including desks, printers, and whiteboards, in various departments.
Retrieve system configuration details to verify automatic logout and screen lock settings.
Collect a list of printers and their configurations (to verify if they have an authentication function).
Gather training records and communication material related to the clear desk and clear screen policy.

Analysis:
For each photographed workspace, verify compliance with the clear desk policy (e.g., no sensitive documents left unattended, no open and unattended endpoint devices).
Assess printer configurations to ensure they align with policy requirements, particularly concerning authentication functions.
Review system configuration details for compliance with automatic logout and screen lock policies.
Evaluate training records and communication materials to ensure adequate dissemination and understanding of the policy.

Output:
A report with photographs of the workspaces assessed.
A spreadsheet or document detailing each printer's configuration and compliance status.
A report on system configurations regarding automatic logout and screen lock features.
A summary of training and communication effectiveness regarding the policy.

controls.title: Sensitive Information Access Management
controls.objective: To ensure that access to sensitive information and associated assets is restricted and managed in accordance with established topic-specific policies.
controls.successCriteria:
All user accounts and their respective access levels conform to the specifications in the Access Matrix.
All change requests related to access permissions are properly documented and executed following established procedures.
No evidence of unauthorized access or improper modification of access levels.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the Access Control List (ACL) or Access Matrix documenting permissions for systems containing sensitive information.
Gather screenshots or system logs showing current user accounts and their access levels on these systems.
Collect recent change requests related to user access or permissions since the last audit.

Analysis:
For each user account on the system, verify that the Access Matrix authorizes their access level.
Review the change requests to confirm they align with the established process for modifying access permissions.
Examine system logs or screenshots to check for any unauthorized access or anomalies in access patterns.

Output:
A report or spreadsheet cross-referencing each user account with the Access Matrix to validate proper access levels.
A detailed review of each change request, confirming adherence to the access modification procedures.
Anomalies or irregularities report highlighting any unauthorized access or deviations from expected access patterns.

controls.title: Personal Information Accuracy and Relevance
controls.objective: To ensure the collection and maintenance of personal information is accurate, up-to-date, complete, and relevant.
controls.successCriteria:
Personal information across systems is accurate, up-to-date, complete, and relevant.
Effective processes are in place for regular updates and verification of personal information.
Compliance with policies and regulations regarding the accuracy and relevance of personal data.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a sample of personal information records from various systems.
Collect documentation of the processes for updating and verifying personal information.
Access logs of recent updates or changes to personal information.

Analysis:
Review the sampled records to assess their accuracy and completeness.
Analyze the processes for maintaining and verifying up-to-date and relevant information.
Examine update logs to evaluate the frequency and effectiveness of data updates.

Output:
A report on the accuracy and completeness of personal information records.
Analysis of the effectiveness of maintenance and verification processes.
Documentation highlighting any identified gaps in data accuracy or relevance.

controls.title: Incident Management Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that the organization's Incident Management process aligns with established policies and effectively manages information security incidents from identification through to resolution and learning.
controls.successCriteria:
The Incident Management process is fully compliant with the policy and IRP.
All incidents are appropriately categorized, assessed, contained, escalated, and resolved in line with the policy.
Communication and notification procedures are executed effectively and within legal and contractual timeframes.
Post-incident analyses lead to continuous improvements in the incident management process.
Incident response personnel receive adequate and up-to-date training and professional development.
Evidence collection and handling procedures are legally compliant and adhere to internal standards.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the Incident Management Policy documentation, including the Incident Response Plan (IRP).
Collect records of recent information security incidents, including their categorization, assessment, and response documentation.
Gather logs of incident containment, escalation, and coordination activities.
Acquire evidence of incident-related communications to stakeholders and authorities.
Obtain documentation of post-incident analyses and lessons learned.
Secure evidence of training and professional development activities for incident response personnel.
Retrieve audit logs for evidence collection procedures related to information security events.

Analysis:
Review the Incident Management Policy and IRP to verify that they comprehensively cover all aspects of incident management as per your requirements.
Analyze records of recent incidents to ensure they were identified, categorized, contained, escalated, and resolved according to the policy.
Evaluate communication logs to verify timely and appropriate stakeholder notification.
Assess post-incident analysis documents to check for root cause analysis and learning integration into the incident management process.
Review training and development records of incident response personnel to ensure compliance with policy requirements.
Analyze evidence collection procedures to validate adherence to legal and internal standards.

Output:
A comprehensive report assessing the alignment of the Incident Management process with the policy.
A spreadsheet or database documenting the review of recent incidents, including categorization, response, and resolution.
Documentation of communication and notification actions taken during incidents.
Analysis of post-incident reviews and implemented improvements.
Records of training and professional development activities for incident response personnel.
Audit trail of evidence collection and handling procedures.

controls.title: Management Responsibilities Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that management and staff comply with information security policies, and management supports and enforces these policies effectively.
controls.successCriteria:
Management Support Verification: Clear evidence of management's active support and endorsement of information security policies.
Training Completion: All staff have undergone regular information security awareness training relevant to their roles.
Effective Reporting Channels: Confidential reporting channels are functional, accessible, and well-documented.
Comprehensive Staff Guidelines and Briefings: All staff have received appropriate guidelines and briefings on their information security roles and responsibilities.
Adequate Resource Allocation: Sufficient resources and planning time are allocated for implementing security-related processes and controls.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Management Communications: Collect recent communications (e.g., emails, memos) from management endorsing information security policies.
Training Records: Obtain records of information security awareness training and professional education programs.
Reporting Channel Documentation: Get documentation on the confidential reporting channels provided for information security policy violations.
Staff Guidelines and Briefing Records: Gather records showing guidelines provided to staff regarding information security expectations and briefings on their roles and responsibilities.
Resource Allocation Records: Access records demonstrating resources and time allocated for implementing security-related processes and controls.

Analysis:
Review of Management Communications: Examine management communications for evidence of active support and endorsement of information security policies.
Training Records Examination: Check training records to ensure staff are regularly trained and aware of their information security responsibilities.
Evaluation of Reporting Channels: Assess the functionality and accessibility of the confidential reporting channels.
Staff Guidelines and Briefing Compliance: Verify that staff members have received appropriate guidelines and briefings on their information security roles and responsibilities.
Resource Allocation Assessment: Evaluate the adequacy of resources and time allocated for security-related processes and controls.

Output:
Report on Management Communications: Summarize findings from the review of management's support for information security policies.
Training Compliance Spreadsheet: List of staff members with corresponding records of completed training sessions.
Reporting Channel Evaluation Report: Assessment of the confidentiality, accessibility, and effectiveness of the reporting channels.
Staff Guidelines and Briefing Compliance Report: Documentation showing compliance with the provision of guidelines and briefings to staff.
Resource Allocation Evaluation Report: Assessment of resources and time allocation for security processes and controls.

controls.title: Personnel Screening and Verification Control
controls.objective: To ensure thorough and ongoing background verification checks are carried out for all personnel before and during their tenure, in compliance with relevant laws and proportionate to their roles and risks.
controls.successCriteria:
All personnel have undergone the required level of screening before and during their employment.
Screening processes are in line with legal, ethical, and business requirements.
Continuous suitability of personnel is periodically reassessed, especially for those in critical roles.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all personnel (full-time, part-time, temporary, and contractors) hired since the last audit.
Gather all background verification reports and relevant screening documents for these personnel.
Collect documentation on any ongoing screening processes for existing employees.
Retrieve copies of contractual agreements with suppliers, specifically clauses related to screening requirements.

Analysis:
Review the background verification reports and screening documents for completeness and compliance with organizational policy and legal requirements.
Verify the implementation of screening processes, including checks for references, academic and professional qualifications, and identity verification.
For personnel in critical roles, ensure additional verifications like credit reviews or criminal record checks have been conducted.
Examine supplier contracts to confirm inclusion and adherence to screening requirements.
Assess procedures and criteria for periodic re-verification of personnel suitability.

Output:
A report detailing the compliance status of each personnel’s background verification.
A compliance matrix comparing screening requirements against actual checks performed.
An analysis of supplier contracts regarding screening clauses.

controls.title: PII Privacy Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that the organization's handling of PII aligns with its privacy policy, applicable laws, and contractual requirements.
controls.successCriteria:
All systems and processes comply with the organization's Privacy Policy.
PII handling procedures are effectively implemented and align with regulatory requirements.
Privacy incidents and complaints are managed promptly and effectively, with measures in place to prevent recurrence.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve the organization’s Privacy Policy document.
Obtain a list of all systems and processes that handle PII.
Gather evidence of PII handling procedures, such as process documentation, training records, or system configuration settings.
Collect records of any recent privacy-related incidents or complaints.

Analysis:
Review the Privacy Policy to ensure it covers all aspects of PII handling as per legal and contractual requirements.
Cross-reference the list of systems and processes with the policy to ensure all PII handling methods are accounted for and compliant.
Examine evidence of PII handling procedures to verify that they are implemented as per the policy and meet regulatory standards.
Assess incident and complaint records to evaluate how privacy breaches are managed and resolved.

Output:
A report detailing the compliance of each system and process with the Privacy Policy.
A summary of the effectiveness of PII handling procedures.
An analysis of incident and complaint handling related to PII.

controls.title: CVV Retention Prevention
controls.objective: Ensure that the card verification code (CVV) is not stored in any systems post-authorization to comply with PCI DSS requirements and protect sensitive cardholder data.
controls.successCriteria:
No evidence of CVV storage post-authorization in any of the Banking & Accounting systems.
Compliance with PCI DSS requirements regarding sensitive cardholder data protection.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of systems classified under "Banking & Accounting" from the Access Matrix.
Acquire screenshots of payment processing interfaces in these systems.
Gather logs or records of transactions processed since the last audit, with a focus on data retention practices.

Analysis:
For each transaction record, verify that no CVV data is stored post-authorization.
Examine payment processing interfaces in screenshots to ensure there is no functionality or field for storing CVVs.
Cross-reference transaction logs with system capabilities to ensure compliance with CVV retention policies.

Output:
Compiled list of audited systems with annotations on CVV handling.
Screenshots of payment processing interfaces.
Analysis report of transaction logs and data retention practices.

controls.title: DHCP Inventory Update
controls.objective: Ensure that DHCP logging is effectively used to maintain an accurate and up-to-date enterprise asset inventory.
controls.successCriteria:
The enterprise asset inventory is updated at least weekly, incorporating new and changed assets identified in DHCP logs.
The updated asset inventory accurately reflects the current state of enterprise assets as determined from DHCP logs.
Asset inventory update processes are consistently followed and documented.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve DHCP logs from all DHCP servers or IP address management tools.
Obtain the current enterprise asset inventory list.
Gather records of asset inventory updates for the last month.

Analysis:
Analyze DHCP logs to identify new or changed assets within the network.
Compare the identified assets from DHCP logs with the current enterprise asset inventory to verify updates.
Review the frequency and comprehensiveness of asset inventory updates.

Output:
Compiled list of assets identified from DHCP logs.
Comparison report between DHCP identified assets and enterprise asset inventory.
Documentation of asset inventory update records.

controls.title: Stakeholder Coordination in Incident Response
controls.objective: Ensure effective coordination with internal and external stakeholders, including law enforcement, during incident response activities. This includes establishing protocols for voluntary information sharing with external stakeholders to enhance broader cybersecurity situational awareness.
controls.successCriteria:
The IRP explicitly includes protocols for coordination with internal and external stakeholders, including practices for voluntary information sharing.
Incident records show consistent and effective engagement with stakeholders and demonstrate information sharing activities as per the IRP.
Communication logs and records demonstrate clear, timely, and appropriate coordination and information sharing efforts.
Existing agreements or protocols with external entities, like law enforcement agencies, are adhered to during incident response, including aspects of voluntary information sharing.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the Incident Response Plan (IRP) document.
Gather a record of recent security incidents and their corresponding response activities.
Collect communication logs or records showing coordination efforts with internal and external stakeholders (emails, meeting minutes, joint task force reports, etc.).
Acquire documentation of any agreements or protocols established with law enforcement agencies or other external entities for information sharing.
Obtain records of voluntary information sharing instances with external stakeholders for cybersecurity situational awareness.

Analysis:
Review the IRP to confirm the inclusion of protocols for stakeholder coordination, focusing on roles, responsibilities, and procedures for engaging with internal and external entities, including voluntary information sharing practices.
For each recorded incident, assess whether coordination with stakeholders and information sharing activities were executed as per the IRP.
Examine communication logs or records to verify actual engagement and information sharing with stakeholders, ensuring alignment with the IRP and any established agreements or protocols.
Evaluate the efficiency and effectiveness of the coordination and information sharing efforts in terms of response time, communication clarity, stakeholder involvement, and contribution to broader cybersecurity situational awareness.

Output:
A compliance report detailing how each incident was managed concerning stakeholder coordination and information sharing.
An evaluation report of communication logs, coordination efforts, and information sharing instances.
An assessment of the IRP's current stakeholder coordination and information sharing protocols.

controls.title: Post-Termination Information Security Responsibilities
controls.objective: To ensure that information security responsibilities and duties of personnel are adhered to and enforced even after the termination or change of their employment.
controls.successCriteria:
All termination/change of employment agreements include required clauses on post-employment information security responsibilities.
Documented evidence shows that departing employees were made aware of their ongoing information security responsibilities.
The compliance matrix demonstrates full alignment of agreement clauses with policy standards.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of recent employment terminations and role changes from the HR department.
Collect copies of exit interview documentation and termination/change of employment agreements.
Retrieve relevant sections from the employee handbook or policies that outline post-termination duties and responsibilities.

Analysis:
Review termination/change of employment agreements to ensure they include clauses related to post-employment information security responsibilities.
Compare the outlined responsibilities in the agreements with the policy standards to verify alignment and completeness.
Assess the exit interview documentation to confirm that information security responsibilities were communicated and understood by the departing employees.

Output:
A report detailing the review of termination/change of employment agreements.
A compliance matrix comparing agreement clauses against policy standards.
Summary of findings from exit interview document reviews.

controls.title: Disciplinary Process Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure adherence to the formal disciplinary process for information security policy violations.
controls.successCriteria:
The disciplinary process for each reported violation aligns with the Information Security Policy.
Actions taken in response to violations are appropriate based on the nature and severity of the breach, as well as the training and intent of the violator.
Consistent application of the disciplinary process across all cases.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a record of all reported information security policy violations.
Collect documentation detailing the initiation and conclusion of each disciplinary process.
Retrieve training records of the individuals involved in the violations.

Analysis:
Review each reported violation to confirm if the disciplinary process was initiated as per policy.
For each disciplinary action, assess whether the response was graduated based on the nature and gravity of the breach, the intention behind the offense, and the training status of the violator.
Compare the actions taken against policy violators with the guidelines set in the Information Security Policy to ensure consistency and fairness.

Output:
A report summarizing each violation and the corresponding disciplinary action.
A comparison matrix showing the alignment between each case's specifics and the response as per the policy.
A summary of discrepancies, if any, between the policy and its implementation.

controls.title: Business Continuity Exercise Programme
controls.objective: To validate the effectiveness of business continuity strategies and solutions through regular and comprehensive exercising and testing.
controls.successCriteria:
All exercises are conducted as per the planned schedule and following significant changes.
Scenarios used are relevant and aligned with business continuity objectives.
Exercises effectively develop the required skills and knowledge in participants.
Post-exercise reports are comprehensive and actionable.
Documented improvements and changes are implemented based on exercise findings.
Continuous improvement is evident through periodic reviews and updates to the exercise programme.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:

Obtain the exercise and testing schedule for the current year.
Gather the list of scenarios used for each exercise.
Collect pre-exercise plans, including aims and objectives.
Retrieve post-exercise reports, including outcomes, recommendations, and actions.
Obtain records of changes implemented based on exercise outcomes.
Review team feedback and competence assessments post-exercise.

Analysis:

Verify that exercises are conducted according to the schedule and any significant organizational changes.
Assess if the scenarios used are appropriate and align with business continuity objectives.
Check if exercises develop teamwork, competence, confidence, and knowledge for relevant roles.
Ensure post-exercise reports are formalized and include outcomes, recommendations, and actions.
Review if exercises validate business continuity strategies and solutions over time.
Confirm that results are reviewed and lead to continual improvements.

Output:

Schedule and attendance records for each exercise.
Documentation of exercise scenarios and plans.
Post-exercise reports with detailed outcomes, recommendations, and actions.
Records of changes implemented following exercise findings.
Team feedback and competence assessment results.

controls.title: Internal Responsibility Accountability
controls.objective: To ensure individuals are held accountable for their internal control responsibilities in pursuit of organizational objectives.
controls.successCriteria:
All roles and responsibilities within the organization are clearly defined and aligned with internal control objectives.
Performance evaluations effectively measure compliance with internal control responsibilities.
Incentives and rewards are appropriately aligned with internal control compliance and the achievement of organizational objectives.
Disciplinary actions are effectively enforced in cases of policy violations or incidents, demonstrating an effective accountability mechanism.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the organizational structure chart highlighting individual roles and responsibilities.
Collect performance evaluation reports focusing on internal control responsibilities.
Gather documentation of incentives, rewards, and disciplinary actions related to internal control compliance.
Retrieve records of any privacy policy violations or related incidents.

Analysis:
Review the organizational chart to verify the clarity of internal control responsibilities across different roles.
Analyze performance reports to assess whether internal control responsibilities are being evaluated effectively.
Examine incentives and rewards to ensure they align with internal control compliance and organizational objectives.
Investigate any instances of policy violations or incidents to evaluate the effectiveness of the disciplinary process.

Output:
A report summarizing the alignment of roles and responsibilities with internal control objectives.
A spreadsheet or document analyzing the effectiveness of performance evaluations in internal control compliance.
A summary of the incentives and disciplinary actions, highlighting their effectiveness and alignment with internal control objectives.

controls.title: Passive Asset Discovery and Inventory Update
controls.objective: To ensure all assets connected to the enterprise's network are identified and the asset inventory is updated at least weekly using a passive discovery tool.
controls.successCriteria:
The passive asset discovery tool is configured and functioning to scan the network at least weekly.
All assets identified in the scans are accurately reflected in the updated asset inventory.
Asset inventory updates are consistently logged and align with the scans conducted by the tool.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the latest asset inventory report generated by the passive asset discovery tool.
Get the configuration settings or logs from the passive asset discovery tool to confirm scan frequency.
Collect change logs or update records of the asset inventory for the period since the last audit.

Analysis:
Verify that the passive asset discovery tool is configured to scan the network at least weekly.
Compare the assets identified in the latest scan with the updated asset inventory to ensure all new or changed assets are accurately reflected.
Review the change logs or update records to confirm that the asset inventory is updated following each scan.

Output:
Latest asset inventory report.
Configuration settings or logs from the passive asset discovery tool.
Change logs or update records of the asset inventory.

controls.title: Email Gateway File Type Filtering
controls.objective: To prevent unnecessary or potentially harmful file types from entering the organization's network through the email gateway, enhancing overall email security.
controls.successCriteria:
The email gateway is configured correctly to filter out unauthorized file types.
The logs demonstrate effective blocking of unauthorized file types and allowance of only approved file types.
The list of approved file types aligns with the file types observed in the gateway logs.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the configuration settings of the email gateway system.
Gather recent logs from the email gateway showing blocked and allowed file types.
Retrieve a list of approved file types permitted through the email gateway.

Analysis:
Assess the email gateway settings to verify the implementation of file type filtering.
Review the logs to confirm the blocking of unauthorized file types and the allowance of approved file types.
Compare the list of approved file types with the email gateway settings to ensure alignment.

Output:
A report detailing the configuration settings of the email gateway.
A log analysis report showing blocked and allowed file types.
A comparison report between approved file types and those observed in the logs.

controls.title: Logging Management
controls.objective: To ensure comprehensive logging of activities, exceptions, faults, and relevant events across all systems, and safeguard the integrity and confidentiality of log data.
controls.successCriteria:
All audited systems are logging events as per policy requirements, capturing necessary details.
Log data is securely stored, protected from unauthorized access or manipulation, and retained according to the defined policies.
Regular log reviews are conducted, with anomalies investigated and appropriately actioned.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve a current list of all logging systems and applications in use (from the IT Asset Inventory).
Obtain recent log files from a sample of critical systems (e.g., servers, network devices, applications).
Gather documentation on log configuration settings and log retention policies.
Collect evidence of log analysis activities, such as reports or summaries of log reviews.

Analysis:
Verify that each system's logs include necessary details such as user IDs, system activities, event times, device identities, network addresses, and protocols.
Assess the configuration settings against the logging policy to ensure compliance with data collection, storage, and protection requirements.
Examine log analysis reports to check for regular review, identification of anomalies, and follow-up actions.
Cross-reference log retention policies with actual log files to confirm adherence to retention schedules.

Output:
A report summarizing the logging configurations and practices of each audited system.
A compliance matrix comparing current practices against policy requirements.
Analysis reports of log reviews, highlighting any discrepancies or concerns.

controls.title: Change Management Oversight
controls.objective: Ensure that all changes to information processing facilities and information systems adhere to the established change management procedures.
controls.successCriteria:
All changes to information processing facilities and information systems are fully documented, authorized, and comply with the established change management procedures.
Operating documentation and ICT continuity plans are updated in accordance with the changes made.
There are no instances of non-compliance or unauthorized changes.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a comprehensive list of all information processing facilities and information systems.
Collect the complete record of change requests and change records since the last audit.
Acquire detailed documentation of change management procedures, including planning, authorization, testing, and implementation processes.

Analysis:
Verify that each change record includes planning, impact assessment, authorization, testing, and implementation details.
Assess whether changes communicated to relevant parties are in line with the change management procedures.
Examine the documentation for evidence of emergency and contingency planning, including fall-back procedures.
Ensure that operating documentation and ICT continuity plans have been updated as per the changes made.

Output:
A detailed report outlining the compliance of each change record with the change management procedures.
A summary of discrepancies or non-compliance issues, if any.
An updated list of all changes, categorizing them into minor, major, and emergency changes.

controls.title: Compliance Review Control
controls.objective: To ensure regular review of compliance with the organization's information security policy, specific policies, rules, and standards.
controls.successCriteria:
All aspects of the information security policy and specific policies are regularly reviewed and complied with.
Effective corrective actions are implemented in response to identified non-compliances.
Continuous improvement in the compliance review process with effective utilization of automatic tools, where applicable.
controls.testingMethodology:
