Evidence:
Obtain the latest versions of the organization’s information security policy and topic-specific policies.
Gather records of the previous compliance reviews, including any instances of non-compliance and corrective actions taken.
Collect reports of automatic measurement and reporting tools used for compliance review (if applicable).
Retrieve records of managerial actions taken in response to non-compliance, including corrective action plans and their outcomes.

Analysis:
Review the compliance review records to assess adherence to the information security policy and specific policies.
Examine instances of non-compliance to identify trends or recurring issues.
Evaluate the effectiveness of corrective actions taken in response to previous instances of non-compliance.
Assess the utilization and effectiveness of automatic measurement and reporting tools in the compliance review process.
Analyze managerial reports for thoroughness in addressing non-compliance and the efficacy of corrective actions.

Output:
A summary report detailing the findings of the compliance review.
A list of any current non-compliances and recommendations for corrective actions.
An evaluation report on the effectiveness of previously implemented corrective actions.
Recommendations for improvements in the compliance review process or policy adjustments, if needed.

controls.title: Audit Log Retention Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure that audit logs across all enterprise assets are retained for a minimum of 90 days to comply with regulatory and organizational standards.
controls.successCriteria:
All enterprise assets are configured to retain logs for at least 90 days.
Sampled log entries demonstrate compliance with the 90-day retention period.
Log integrity and confidentiality are adequately maintained.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain an inventory of all enterprise assets (servers, workstations, network devices, applications, etc.).
Collect configuration settings or reports from logging mechanisms (like SIEM systems) to verify log retention settings.
Gather a sample of log entries from various systems to verify the earliest log entry date.

Analysis:
For each asset, verify that log retention settings are configured to retain logs for at least 90 days.
Examine the sample log entries to ensure logs cover the required retention period.
Review system configurations to ensure that log integrity and confidentiality are maintained during the retention period.

Output:
Configuration reports or screenshots showing log retention settings.
Spreadsheet or report detailing log entry dates for each sampled system.
Documentation of log integrity and confidentiality measures.

controls.title: Unauthorized Asset Management
controls.objective: To ensure that all assets on the network are authorized and compliant with company policies. Unauthorized assets should be identified and addressed on a weekly basis.
controls.successCriteria:
All unauthorized assets are accurately identified and reported.
Each unauthorized asset is assessed for risk and appropriate action is taken (e.g., removal from the network, denial of remote access, or quarantine).
Documentation of actions taken for each unauthorized asset is maintained.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a current inventory list of all network-connected assets.
Run a network scan to identify all connected assets.
Obtain logs of network access and security alerts related to unauthorized assets.

Analysis:
Compare the network scan results with the inventory list to identify unauthorized assets.
Analyze network access logs and security alerts to confirm any unauthorized asset activity.
Determine the status (active/inactive) and risk level of each identified unauthorized asset.

Output:
A report listing all unauthorized assets identified during the scan.
A log analysis report highlighting unauthorized asset activities.
A risk assessment report for each unauthorized asset.

controls.title: ISMS Review and Monitoring
controls.objective: Validate the effectiveness and updates of the ISMS.
controls.successCriteria: ISMS is regularly reviewed, audited, and updated as necessary.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence: 
Records of internal audits, ISMS reviews, and related findings. 
Changes or updates made to the ISMS documentation. 

Analysis: 
Evaluate the frequency and results of audits and reviews. 

Output: 
Overview of ISMS performance and areas of improvement.

controls.title: Disclosure Record-Keeping and Monitoring
controls.objective: To ensure complete, accurate, and timely records of authorized and unauthorized disclosures of personal information.
controls.successCriteria:
All authorized disclosures are accurately and completely recorded and comply with internal policies.
Records of unauthorized disclosures are complete, timely, and aligned with incident reports.
The organization's record-keeping practices meet data protection regulatory requirements.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the record logs of all disclosures of personal information.
Access incident reports related to unauthorized disclosures or breaches.
Gather policy documents regarding disclosure procedures and incident response.

Analysis:
Cross-check the disclosure logs against internal policies to verify authorized disclosures.
Review incident reports to assess the accuracy and completeness of unauthorized disclosure records.
Compare records with data protection regulations to ensure compliance in record-keeping.

Output:
A comprehensive report on all recorded disclosures of personal information.
Analysis summary of incident reports and response effectiveness.
Compliance assessment with internal and regulatory standards.

controls.title: Audit Testing Security Control
controls.objective: To ensure that audit testing of information systems is conducted securely, minimizing the risk to operational integrity and confidentiality.
controls.successCriteria:
All audit tests are conducted within the agreed scope and access limitations.
Devices used for audit purposes meet established security requirements.
Auditor access to systems and data is appropriately monitored and logged.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a schedule of planned audit tests, including details of the systems and data to be accessed.
Collect written agreements between auditors and management regarding the scope and access level of audit tests.
Get evidence of security measures for devices used in audits (e.g., antivirus status, patch levels).
Acquire copies of logs recording auditor access to systems and data.

Analysis:
Verify that the scope of each audit test matches the agreed terms.
Confirm that auditor access to systems and data is appropriately limited (e.g., read-only access, use of isolated copies of system files).
Check that security requirements for devices used in audits are met.
Review logs to ensure that auditor access is properly monitored and recorded.

Output:
A report detailing the adherence of each audit test to the agreed terms.
Verification documentation for the security status of devices used in audits.
A comprehensive log review report.

controls.title: Email Authentication and Verification
controls.objective: Ensure the organization's email system adheres to DMARC, SPF, and DKIM standards to mitigate risks associated with spoofed or modified emails.
controls.successCriteria:
SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configurations are accurately implemented as per organizational standards.
Email traffic log analysis shows a reduction in spoofed or unauthorized email activities.
Email security policies and procedures are consistently followed and documented.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a current configuration report of the email system.
Gather DNS records showing SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configurations.
Collect recent email traffic logs for analysis.
Retrieve documented email security policies and procedures.

Analysis:
Verify that SPF records are correctly set up in DNS to authenticate outbound emails from the organization's domain.
Ensure DKIM signatures are applied on outbound emails, and the corresponding DKIM DNS records are properly configured.
Check that DMARC policies are defined in DNS and align with organizational security policies, particularly for handling failed email authentications.
Review email traffic logs to identify instances of email spoofing or authentication failures and assess the effectiveness of the DMARC policy.

Output:
Report on SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configuration settings.
Analysis of email traffic logs with identified spoofing or authentication issues.
Compliance assessment report with email security policies and procedures.

controls.title: Utility Protection and Resilience
controls.objective: To ensure that information processing facilities are adequately protected from disruptions caused by utility failures, including power, telecommunications, and other essential services.
controls.successCriteria:
All utility support equipment is configured, operated, and maintained as per manufacturer specifications.
Regular maintenance and inspection of utility support equipment are conducted and documented.
Effective network segregation is in place for utility support systems.
Emergency response plans are comprehensive, up-to-date, and effective in handling utility disruptions.
Redundant and diverse utility feeds are implemented where necessary for critical utilities.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a detailed inventory of all utility support equipment (e.g., generators, UPS, HVAC systems) used in the information processing facilities.
Acquire maintenance and inspection records for the utility support equipment.
Gather documentation on the configuration and network segregation of utility support systems.
Collect evidence of the implementation of diverse utility feeds, where applicable.
Obtain emergency response plans, including details of emergency lighting, communication systems, and location of emergency cut-off switches and valves.

Analysis:
Assess the inventory of utility support equipment against manufacturer specifications for proper configuration, operation, and maintenance.
Review the maintenance and inspection records to verify regular and appropriate servicing of the utility support equipment.
Examine the documentation on network segregation to ensure utility equipment is appropriately separated from the information processing network.
Analyze the implementation of diverse utility feeds for redundancy and resilience.
Evaluate the emergency response plans for completeness and effectiveness in handling utility disruptions.

Output:
A report detailing the status of utility support equipment and their compliance with manufacturer specifications.
A compliance checklist against the regular maintenance and inspection of utility support equipment.
A review report on network segregation and security measures for utility support systems.
An evaluation report of emergency response plans and redundancy measures.

controls.title: POI Device Inspection
controls.objective: Ensure Point of Interaction (POI) devices are regularly inspected for tampering or unauthorized substitution to maintain security and integrity.
controls.successCriteria:
All POI devices are accounted for in the inventory and have up-to-date inspection logs.
Inspection logs demonstrate thorough examination for tampering and unauthorized substitution.
No evidence of tampering or unauthorized substitution is found, or if found, appropriate actions are documented and resolved.
Inspection personnel are well-informed about the procedures and consistently adhere to them.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a comprehensive inventory list of all POI devices from the Asset Management System.
Collect inspection logs and reports documenting periodic inspections of POI device surfaces.
Gather statements or acknowledgments from personnel responsible for conducting the inspections.

Analysis:
Verify each POI device in the inventory has corresponding inspection logs, ensuring regular and timely inspections.
Review inspection logs and reports for any signs of tampering, unauthorized substitution, or attachment of skimming devices.
Assess the awareness and adherence of inspection personnel to the established inspection procedures.
Examine any reported incidents or discrepancies noted during inspections for adequate follow-up and resolution.

Output:
A comprehensive spreadsheet linking each POI device to its latest inspection report.
A summary of findings from the inspection reports, highlighting any irregularities or concerns.
Documentation of follow-up actions taken for any incidents of tampering or unauthorized substitution detected.

controls.title: Records Protection and Management Control
controls.objective: To ensure the authenticity, reliability, integrity, and usability of organizational records throughout their lifecycle, including creation, storage, access, and disposal.
controls.successCriteria:
Records are managed and protected according to the organization’s guidelines.
The retention schedule is strictly followed for all record types.
No unauthorized access or manipulation of records is detected.
Electronic storage systems are adequate to ensure record availability and integrity over time.
Effective management of cryptographic keys and programs for the duration of the records’ retention period.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the organization’s records management guidelines and retention schedule.
Gather a sample of records from various categories (e.g., HR, financial, legal) and their storage media.
Access logs and change management records for these sample records.
Collect details of the systems used for electronic record storage, including any cryptographic keys and programs.

Analysis:
Review the records management guidelines against the provided requirement for comprehensiveness.
Verify that the retention schedule is being followed for the sample records.
Check the access logs for any unauthorized access or manipulation.
Assess the electronic storage systems for adequacy in preserving records’ readability and accessibility over time.
Ensure cryptographic keys and programs for encrypted archives are adequately managed.

Output:
A report on compliance with records management guidelines.
A spreadsheet or document comparing each sampled record against its retention schedule and handling requirements.
An analysis report on the security and adequacy of electronic storage systems.
A review report on the management of cryptographic keys and programs.

controls.title: Authority Contact Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure timely and appropriate contact with relevant authorities and adherence to regulatory requirements in the event of information security incidents.
controls.successCriteria:
All necessary contacts with authorities in response to information security incidents are made in accordance with the established policy.
Regular and effective communication with special interest groups is maintained.
The organization demonstrates awareness and understanding of current and upcoming information security regulations as applicable.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a record of all reported information security incidents from the Incident Management log.
Gather documentation of communications with relevant authorities related to these incidents.
Access the policy document outlining the protocol for contacting authorities in the event of an information security incident.
Collect records of regular updates and communications with special interest groups.

Analysis:
Review each reported incident to determine if it necessitated contact with authorities according to the policy guidelines.
For each incident where contact was required, verify that the relevant authorities were appropriately notified.
Assess the regularity and relevance of communications and consultations with special interest groups, ensuring alignment with current and upcoming regulatory expectations.

Output:
A report listing all reviewed incidents and the corresponding actions taken.
Documentation verifying communications with authorities and special interest groups.
An assessment report on the adherence to the policy for contacting authorities.

controls.title: Documented Procedures Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure that all information processing facilities operate in accordance with documented procedures to maintain information security.
controls.successCriteria:
All operational activities conform to the documented operating procedures.
Documented procedures are regularly reviewed, updated, and authorized.
Responses to errors and system failures are in line with the documented procedures.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the current list of documented operating procedures.
Collect evidence of operating procedure implementations, such as system configuration records, backup logs, and error handling reports.
Gather recent updates or changes made to the operating procedures.

Analysis:
For each operational activity, verify that the existing implementation aligns with the documented procedures.
Review the update history of the operating procedures to ensure they are regularly reviewed and authorized.
Examine instances of error handling and system failures to assess if the responses adhered to the documented procedures.

Output:
A comprehensive report comparing the actual operational practices against the documented procedures.
A log of updates and changes to the operating procedures, along with authorization records.
An analysis report of error handling and system failure incidents.

controls.title: Storage Media Lifecycle Management
controls.objective: Ensure the secure management of storage media throughout its lifecycle, including acquisition, use, transportation, and disposal, in alignment with the organization's classification scheme and handling requirements.
controls.successCriteria:
The policy for storage media management is comprehensive and adhered to.
All storage media acquisitions, disposals, and transportations are documented and authorized.
Usage and removal of storage media are properly logged and aligned with the audit trail requirements.
Cryptographic measures and environmental protections are adequately implemented.
Regular data transfers to new storage media are conducted to prevent data degradation.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the policy document outlining the management of storage media.
Collect records of storage media acquisitions and disposals.
Gather documentation of storage media transportation, including authorization records.
Acquire logs of storage media usage, removals from premises, and disposal procedures.
Retrieve details of cryptographic measures implemented for information protection on storage media.
Obtain evidence of environmental protection measures for storage media.
Collect records of periodic data transfers to new storage media to avoid degradation.

Analysis:
Review the storage media management policy to ensure it covers all lifecycle stages.
Verify that records of acquisitions and disposals align with policy requirements.
Analyze transportation and authorization records for compliance with the policy.
Assess the logs of usage and removals to ensure adherence to audit trail requirements.
Examine the implementation of cryptographic measures on storage media containing sensitive information.
Evaluate the protection measures against environmental threats.
Analyze the periodic data transfer records to ensure mitigation of degradation risks.

Output:
A report detailing compliance of storage media management with the policy.
A spreadsheet summarizing acquisitions, disposals, transportations, and usage of storage media.
Documentation evidencing cryptographic measures and environmental protections.
Audit findings on the periodic data transfer practices for storage media.

controls.title: Data Leakage Prevention
controls.objective: Ensure the implementation and effective functioning of Data Leakage Prevention measures across all systems, networks, and devices that process, store, or transmit sensitive information.
controls.successCriteria:
All systems, networks, and devices identified are confirmed to have effective DLP measures in place.
DLP tools are correctly configured and operational, with no unauthorized disclosures of sensitive information.
Incidents of data leakage, if any, are adequately addressed with appropriate response and remediation actions.
Strict adherence to user access controls and data transfer policies is maintained.
Training and awareness programs are effective in educating employees about data leakage risks and prevention.
Backups of sensitive information are adequately protected with robust encryption and access controls.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all systems, networks, and devices used for processing, storing, and transmitting sensitive information.
Acquire configuration details and operational logs of Data Leakage Prevention (DLP) tools deployed in the organization.
Collect recent incident reports and logs pertaining to data leakage events, if any.
Retrieve documentation on user access controls related to copying, pasting, and uploading of data to external services.
Gather evidence of training and awareness programs conducted for employees on data leakage prevention.
Obtain backup policies and evidence of encryption and access controls for backed-up data.

Analysis:
Verify that all listed systems, networks, and devices are equipped with appropriate DLP measures.
Analyze DLP tool configuration and operational logs to ensure sensitive data is being correctly identified, monitored, and protected against unauthorized disclosure.
Review incident reports and logs for any data leakage events and assess the response and remediation actions taken.
Examine user access controls to ensure compliance with policies on data transfer restrictions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of training and awareness programs in imparting knowledge on data leakage risks and prevention.
Assess the adequacy of encryption and access controls applied to backed-up data, ensuring sensitive information is adequately protected.

Output:
A comprehensive report detailing the DLP measures in place for each system, network, and device.
Analysis report of DLP tool effectiveness and incident handling.
Assessment report on user access controls and compliance with data transfer restrictions.
Training and awareness program effectiveness report.
Backup policy compliance report, with a focus on encryption and access control measures.

controls.title: Centralized AAA Management
controls.objective: Ensure that network Authentication, Authorization, and Auditing (AAA) processes are centralized and consistently applied across all systems and platforms.
controls.successCriteria:
All accounts and their roles are consistent with the Access Matrix.
All change requests have been executed following the established procedures and authorization protocols.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the Access Matrix listing all systems with focus on those requiring AAA.
Capture screenshots or reports showing the current state of AAA settings on a sample of these systems.
Gather a list of recent access management change requests.

Analysis:
For each system in the Access Matrix, verify that AAA configurations match the prescribed standards and policies.
For each account shown in the screenshots/reports, ensure it aligns with what the Access Matrix authorizes.
Review all change requests to confirm they adhere to established processes for modifying access rights and AAA configurations.

Output:
A collection of screenshots or system reports.
A spreadsheet or report verifying each account against the Access Matrix.
A report detailing the review of change requests, highlighting any discrepancies or process deviations.

controls.title: Secure SAD Deletion
controls.objective: To ensure that all Sensitive Authentication Data (SAD) is not retained post-authorization and is rendered unrecoverable immediately after the authorization process is complete.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve the list of all systems handling SAD.
Obtain system configurations and logs showing SAD handling and deletion processes.
Acquire documented procedures related to SAD deletion and data retention.

Analysis:
Review system configurations and logs to verify that SAD is not stored post-authorization.
Examine documented procedures to ensure they align with the policy of not retaining SAD after authorization.
Analyze data deletion methods to confirm they render SAD unrecoverable immediately after authorization.

Output:
Report detailing system configurations and logs.
Compliance checklist comparing documented procedures against actual practices.
Analysis report of data deletion methods and their effectiveness.

controls.title: Vulnerability Management Control
controls.objective: To ensure that all technical vulnerabilities within the organization's information systems are identified, evaluated, and appropriately mitigated.
controls.successCriteria:
All information systems are regularly scanned for vulnerabilities, and identified vulnerabilities are promptly addressed.
Updates and patches are effectively applied to mitigate identified vulnerabilities.
Third-party libraries and source codes are regularly reviewed and updated to ensure they do not introduce vulnerabilities into the system.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain an inventory of all information systems including details like software vendor, software name, version numbers, and current deployment state.
Gather recent reports from vulnerability scanning tools.
Collect records of recent penetration tests or vulnerability assessments.
Retrieve documentation of recent software updates or patches applied.
Acquire a list of all third-party libraries and source codes used, along with their vulnerability tracking records.

Analysis:
Cross-reference the system inventory with vulnerability scan reports to verify coverage and identify any unaddressed vulnerabilities.
Review the penetration test or vulnerability assessment reports to assess the effectiveness of the current vulnerability management processes.
For each applied software update or patch, verify that it addresses relevant vulnerabilities and check for successful implementation.
Examine the use of third-party libraries and source codes, ensuring they are up to date and free from known vulnerabilities.

Output:
A comprehensive spreadsheet or report detailing the findings from the vulnerability scans and assessments.
Documentation verifying the successful application of updates and patches.
A report on the status of third-party libraries and source codes, highlighting any potential vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Equipment Maintenance Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure all organizational equipment is maintained according to specified guidelines, preserving the availability, integrity, and confidentiality of information.
controls.successCriteria:
All equipment is maintained as per supplier recommendations and organizational guidelines.
Maintenance is carried out exclusively by authorized personnel, with all activities properly recorded.
Effective security measures are in place for off-premises maintenance, including confidentiality agreements.
Compliance with all maintenance requirements imposed by insurance is achieved.
Equipment inspection post-maintenance confirms no tampering and proper functionality.
Secure disposal or re-use of equipment is conducted as per organizational standards.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a comprehensive list of all equipment under the organization’s maintenance program.
Collect maintenance records, including service schedules, repair logs, and authorization documents for maintenance personnel.
Acquire documentation of any equipment taken off-premises for maintenance, along with associated security measures.
Gather records of maintenance-related compliance with insurance requirements.
Obtain inspection reports of equipment post-maintenance to verify no tampering and proper functionality.
Secure disposal or re-use records for equipment marked for disposal.

Analysis:
Cross-check each piece of equipment against maintenance schedules and records to ensure compliance with supplier recommendations.
Verify that only authorized personnel have conducted maintenance and that all activities are properly documented.
Review security measures and confidentiality agreements related to off-premises maintenance.
Confirm adherence to insurance-mandated maintenance requirements.
Analyze post-maintenance inspection reports to ensure equipment integrity and functionality.
Examine disposal or re-use records for compliance with secure disposal measures.

Output:
A comprehensive spreadsheet or report detailing the maintenance status of each piece of equipment.
Documentation verifying the authorization and qualifications of maintenance personnel.
A report on the security measures for off-premises maintenance.
Compliance reports with insurance-imposed maintenance requirements.
Inspection reports for post-maintenance equipment checks.
Records of secure disposal or re-use of equipment.

controls.title: Active Network Asset Discovery
controls.objective: Ensure all assets connected to the enterprise's network are identified and catalogued daily.
controls.successCriteria:
The Active Discovery Tool identifies all known assets and potential rogue devices daily.
The tool's operation aligns with the configured schedule without any missed executions.
Discrepancies between the tool’s findings and the asset inventory are investigated and resolved.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the daily reports generated by the Active Discovery Tool.
Gather network configuration and topology documentation.
Retrieve logs from the Active Discovery Tool for audit period.

Analysis:
Verify the completeness and accuracy of the Active Discovery Tool's daily reports against network configuration and topology documentation.
Assess the Active Discovery Tool's logs to ensure it operated as scheduled daily.
Cross-check identified assets with the enterprise's asset inventory to identify any unregistered or rogue devices.

Output:
Daily Active Discovery Tool reports.
Comparison spreadsheet matching discovered assets against the official asset inventory.
Log analysis report confirming the tool's operational frequency and accuracy.

controls.title: Web Filtering Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure that access to external websites is effectively managed to minimize exposure to malicious content and adherence to organizational web filtering policies.
controls.successCriteria:
Web filtering tools are configured in compliance with the organizational policy.
No unauthorized access to blocked websites or categories is detected.
All personnel have received and completed training on web filtering and secure use of online resources.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the configuration settings of web filtering tools used on all organizational devices (laptops and mobile phones).
Gather a list of blocked websites and categories as per the web filtering policy.
Collect records of user access logs to external websites.
Acquire training attendance records and materials related to web filtering and secure online resource usage.

Analysis:
Review the configuration settings of web filtering tools to ensure they align with the defined policy (e.g., blocking malicious websites, restricting access to certain categories).
Cross-reference the list of blocked websites and categories with those accessed in user logs to identify any policy violations or lapses in filtering.
Analyze training records to confirm all personnel have received appropriate training on web filtering and secure online resource usage.

Output:
A report detailing the configuration settings of web filtering tools.
A spreadsheet comparing blocked websites/categories with user access logs.
A summary of training records and materials related to web filtering and online resource usage.

controls.title: Intellectual Property Rights Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that the organization's procedures and practices effectively protect its intellectual property rights and comply with relevant laws and internal policies.
controls.successCriteria:
All intellectual property assets are properly documented, licensed, and used in compliance with relevant laws and internal policies.
There are no instances of unauthorized use, duplication, or transfer of intellectual property assets.
Staff are adequately trained and contractually bound to uphold intellectual property rights.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all digital and physical assets that are considered intellectual property (e.g., software, documents, media files).
Gather copies of all software licenses, ownership proofs (like patents, copyrights, trademarks registrations), and user agreements.
Collect recent audit logs and reports related to intellectual property assets.

Analysis:
Verify each asset against the intellectual property register to confirm proper documentation and licensing.
Cross-check the software licenses and user agreements to ensure they are up-to-date and in compliance with the terms.
Review audit logs for unauthorized access, duplication, or transfer of intellectual property assets.
Assess staff training records and contracts for inclusion of intellectual property rights awareness and obligations.

Output:
A verified spreadsheet of all intellectual property assets with corresponding licenses and ownership proofs.
A report on the compliance status of each asset with regards to intellectual property laws and policies.
A summary of staff training and contractual commitments concerning intellectual property rights.

controls.title: Network Security Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure the security of information in networks and protect connected services from unauthorized access.
controls.successCriteria:
Network diagrams and configuration files are current and accurately reflect the network setup.
Network security procedures and controls are effectively implemented and align with organizational policies.
Network security monitoring indicates no unauthorized access and proper handling of security incidents.
Controls for virtualized networks are robust and comply with security standards.
All change requests and executed changes are in accordance with the organization’s change management procedures.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain an updated list of network devices, including routers, switches, and firewalls.
Collect recent network diagrams and configuration files for all network devices.
Gather documentation of network security procedures and controls, including segregation of duties between network and ICT system operations.
Retrieve logs and records of network security monitoring, including logs from firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and access control lists.
Acquire details of implemented controls for virtualized networks, such as SDN and SD-WAN configurations.
Get a record of all change requests related to network security since the last audit.

Analysis:
Review network diagrams and configuration files to confirm they match actual network device settings and are up-to-date.
Evaluate the adequacy of network security procedures and controls, focusing on segregation of duties, authentication mechanisms, and network device hardening.
Analyze network security monitoring logs to identify any anomalies or unauthorized access attempts.
Assess the effectiveness of controls in place for virtualized networks, ensuring logical separation and security measures align with policy.
Compare change requests against executed changes in network configurations to verify adherence to change management procedures.

Output:
Compiled network diagrams and configuration files.
Report on the review of network security procedures and controls.
Analysis of network security monitoring logs.
Evaluation report of virtualized network controls.
Spreadsheet or report correlating change requests with executed changes.

controls.title: Function Segregation and Security Control
controls.objective: Ensure that primary functions with different security levels on the same system component are either isolated from each other, or all functions are secured to the level required by the function with the highest security need. This control aims to prevent primary functions with lower security needs from affecting the security of functions with higher security needs.
controls.successCriteria:
All system components with multiple primary functions are either effectively isolated, or their security measures comply with the highest security level required.
Documentation and configuration analysis confirm that lower security functions do not compromise the security of higher security functions.
Any discrepancies or non-compliances are identified and documented for further action.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all system components hosting multiple primary functions.
Collect configuration details of these system components, including security settings and access controls.
Gather documentation or logs that demonstrate the isolation or security measures implemented for these functions.

Analysis:
Review the list of system components to identify those with multiple primary functions.
For each system component, analyze the configuration details to verify that either isolation is in place, or security settings meet the requirements of the highest security function.
Examine the documentation or logs to validate that the implemented measures effectively segregate or secure the functions as per the criteria.

Output:
A detailed report of system components with multiple primary functions and their respective security configurations.
An analysis report verifying the adequacy of segregation or security measures.
Compliance or discrepancy notes for each system component reviewed.

controls.title: Internal Control Deficiency Assessment and Communication
controls.objective: To ensure timely evaluation and communication of internal control deficiencies to the responsible parties, including senior management and the board of directors, and to monitor the effectiveness of corrective actions.
controls.successCriteria:
All identified internal control deficiencies are assessed and reported accurately.
Communications regarding deficiencies are timely and directed to the appropriate parties.
Corrective actions are effectively implemented and monitored, with progress aligning with the organization's internal control standards.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain reports or records of the latest evaluations of internal control efficiencies.
Gather communications related to identified deficiencies, directed towards responsible parties, senior management, and the board of directors.
Collect documentation on corrective action plans and progress updates.

Analysis:
Review evaluation reports to confirm if internal control deficiencies were identified and assessed appropriately.
Examine communication records to verify that deficiencies were communicated in a timely manner to the correct parties.
Assess corrective action plans and progress reports to ensure deficiencies are being addressed effectively and within a reasonable timeframe.

Output:
Compiled list of identified internal control deficiencies.
Record of communications regarding deficiencies.
Summary report on the status of corrective actions.

controls.title: Malware Protection Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that malware protection measures are effectively implemented and maintained across all IT systems and devices.
controls.successCriteria:
All IT systems and devices have malware protection measures installed, correctly configured, and regularly updated.
Malware scans are conducted as per the prescribed frequency and scope, with appropriate actions taken on any detections.
All users have received and understood malware protection training.
Any instances of disabled malware protection measures are fully compliant with the organizational policy, including proper authorization and justification.
Procedures for obtaining files and software from external sources are robust and effectively mitigate risks of malware introduction.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all IT systems and devices, categorized by type (e.g., endpoint devices, servers, network devices).
Gather current configurations and settings of malware detection and prevention tools across these systems and devices.
Collect recent reports of malware scans and their results.
Acquire records of user awareness training sessions and materials related to malware protection.
Retrieve documentation on procedures for disabling malware protection measures and their justifications, if any.

Analysis:
For each system and device, verify if malware protection tools are installed, correctly configured, and updated regularly.
Compare malware scan reports with the expected frequency and scope of scanning as per the policy.
Review user training records to ensure all users have undergone appropriate malware awareness training.
Analyze the documentation of disabled malware protection measures to confirm they comply with policy requirements, including the authorization and justification process.
Examine the procedures for obtaining files and software from external networks or media to ensure protective measures against malware are in place.

Output:
A detailed report listing the malware protection status of each system and device.
A summary of findings from the review of malware scan reports.
A compliance report on user awareness training related to malware protection.
An assessment report on the authorization and justification of disabled malware protection measures.
An evaluation report on the procedures for external file and software acquisition and their compliance with malware protection standards.

controls.title: Information Security Training Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure all personnel receive appropriate information security training and are aware of information security policies relevant to their job function.
controls.successCriteria:
All personnel have completed the required information security training.
Training materials adequately cover the organization's information security policy, specific policies, and procedures.
Post-training assessments indicate effective knowledge transfer and understanding of information security responsibilities.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the training records database, including records for new hires and existing staff.
Collect documentation of the information security awareness, education, and training program, including materials used (e.g., presentations, e-learning modules).
Gather results of assessments or quizzes conducted after training sessions.

Analysis:
Review the training records to verify that all personnel have completed the required information security training.
Analyze training materials to ensure they cover key topics: organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies, and relevant procedures.
Assess the effectiveness of training by analyzing the results of post-training assessments or quizzes.

Output:
A report detailing the completion status of training for all personnel.
A review summary of the training materials for completeness and relevance.
An analysis report of post-training assessment results.

controls.title: Segregation of Duties Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that duties and responsibilities within the organization are appropriately segregated to prevent conflicts of interest and to maintain the integrity of operations.
controls.successCriteria:
All accounts and their roles must comply with the segregation of duties as outlined in the Access Control Matrix.
All change requests must follow the established process and have proper authorization and justification, ensuring no conflicts of interest or policy breaches.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the Access Control Matrix to identify roles and access rights for systems related to critical functions such as finance, HR, IT, and operations.
Gather screenshots or system reports showing current account roles and access levels for these systems.
Collect a log of change requests for access rights and roles since the last audit, including approvals and justifications.

Analysis:
For each account in the critical systems, verify against the Access Control Matrix to ensure that the roles and access rights are authorized and do not present conflicts of interest.
Review each change request log to confirm that they adhere to the segregation of duties policy, including appropriate approvals and justification for changes.

Output:
Compiled screenshots or system reports of current account roles and access levels.
A spreadsheet or report verifying each account against the Access Control Matrix.
A detailed analysis report of change requests, highlighting compliance or deviations from the policy.

controls.title: Objective Clarity and Risk Assessment Control
controls.objective: To ensure that the entity's objectives are specified with clarity, enabling the identification and assessment of risks relating to these objectives.
controls.successCriteria:
The entity's objectives are clearly documented and reflect management's decisions and industry considerations.
Objectives are in compliance with applicable accounting standards, laws, and regulations.
There is a clear basis for resource allocation in relation to the objectives.
Risk tolerances are adequately documented and aligned with the objectives.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain and review the documented objectives for operations, external financial and non-financial reporting, internal reporting, and compliance.
Acquire evidence of management's decisions on structure, industry considerations, and performance goals.
Collect documentation on tolerances for risk, applicable accounting standards, and relevant laws and regulations.
Gather records of resource allocation based on operations objectives.

Analysis:
Evaluate if the documented objectives reflect management's choices and industry considerations.
Assess whether the objectives consider acceptable levels of risk variation and if they are in compliance with accounting standards and laws.
Analyze the alignment of the objectives with the entity's actual activities and transactions.
Review the basis for resource allocation in relation to the objectives.

Output:
A report detailing the assessment of the alignment and clarity of objectives.
A compliance matrix comparing objectives with applicable standards, laws, and regulations.
Documentation of risk tolerances in relation to each set of objectives.

controls.title: Configuration Management Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that the configurations of hardware, software, services, and networks adhere to the established security standards and templates.
controls.successCriteria:
All hardware, software, services, and network configurations align with the established standard templates.
All configuration changes are properly documented, approved, and comply with the change management process.
The standard configuration templates are up-to-date and effectively address current security requirements and technological changes.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the list of all hardware, software, services, and network assets currently in use.
Gather the most recent versions of the standard configuration templates.
Collect records of configuration changes made since the last audit, including change requests and their implementation details.

Analysis:
For each asset, compare its current configuration with the standard configuration template to identify deviations.
Review the configuration change records to verify that each change:
Was properly documented.
Followed the established process for change management.
Had the necessary approvals.
Assess the templates against new threats, vulnerabilities, and updates in technology to ensure they remain current and comprehensive.

Output:
A report detailing the comparison between each asset's configuration and the standard templates.
A spreadsheet or similar document listing each configuration change, its compliance with the standard process, and approval status.
An evaluation report on the currentness and comprehensiveness of the standard configuration templates.

controls.title: Physical Security Monitoring Control
controls.objective: Ensure continuous monitoring of physical premises to detect and prevent unauthorized access or suspicious behavior using surveillance systems and alarms.
controls.successCriteria:
Complete coverage of critical areas by surveillance systems and alarms.
No evidence of unauthorized access or suspicious behavior in the review period.
Compliance with relevant standards for system testing and maintenance.
Full adherence to local laws and regulations regarding surveillance and data protection.
All physical access to sensitive areas accounted for and authorized.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the list of installed surveillance systems (CCTV, motion detectors, contact detectors, sound sensors) and their locations within the organization’s premises.
Secure access logs from surveillance systems, including video footage, alarm activation records, and system integrity checks.
Collect records of periodic testing and maintenance of surveillance systems and alarms.
Obtain physical access logs to sensitive areas (e.g., server rooms, data centers).
Gather documentation on compliance with local laws and regulations related to surveillance and data protection.

Analysis:
Review surveillance system and alarm locations against the organization's physical security layout to ensure coverage of all critical areas, including external doors and accessible windows.
Analyze access logs and video footage for any signs of unauthorized access or suspicious activities.
Verify the regularity and thoroughness of system testing and maintenance against organizational standards.
Cross-reference physical access logs to sensitive areas with authorized personnel lists to identify any unauthorized access.
Assess compliance with local laws and regulations concerning surveillance, data protection, and retention periods.

Output:
A report detailing the surveillance system and alarm coverage.
Summary of findings from the analysis of access logs and video footage.
Compliance report with local laws and regulations.
Records of system testing and maintenance.
Physical access log audit results.

controls.title: Test Information Security Management
controls.objective: Ensure that test information within development and testing environments is selected, protected, and managed effectively to maintain the confidentiality and integrity of operational information and prevent unauthorized access or use.
controls.successCriteria:
All test environments exhibit robust access control procedures equivalent to those in operational environments.
Each instance of operational information being copied to a test environment is duly authorized and documented.
Comprehensive and accurate logs are maintained for the copying, usage, and deletion of operational information in test environments.
Effective data protection measures (removal or masking) are implemented when sensitive information is used for testing.
Test information is securely stored and strictly used for testing purposes, with no evidence of tampering or unauthorized use.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve a list of all environments where test information is used, including development and testing setups.
Collect documentation or records of authorization for operational information copied to test environments.
Obtain logs detailing the copying, use, and deletion of operational information in test environments.
Gather evidence of data protection measures, such as data masking or removal in test environments.
Secure storage proofs for test information, ensuring it is used solely for testing purposes.

Analysis:
Review the list of environments to verify the application of appropriate access controls, mirroring those in operational environments.
Assess authorization records to confirm that each instance of copying operational information to test environments was properly authorized.
Analyze logs to ensure comprehensive tracking of operational information usage and its subsequent deletion after testing.
Evaluate data protection measures to confirm the safeguarding of sensitive information used in testing.
Inspect storage security measures and usage limitations of test information to validate its protection against tampering and unauthorized use.

Output:
A report summarizing the access control measures in test environments.
A spreadsheet or document listing all authorizations for copying operational information.
A detailed log review report.
An assessment report of data protection measures.
A security analysis report of the storage and usage of test information.

controls.title: Input Validation and Recording
controls.objective: Ensure the completeness and accuracy of system inputs to meet product, service, and reporting requirements.
controls.successCriteria:
All system inputs meet defined requirements for completeness and accuracy.
Validation processes are executed effectively and accurately.
System input activities are recorded completely and accurately in a timely manner.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve lists of system inputs for critical services and products.
Obtain records of input validation processes and results.
Collect logs of system input activities and change requests.

Analysis:
Assess each system input against predefined requirements to confirm compliance.
Review validation process records to ensure thorough and accurate execution.
Analyze logs to verify the completeness and accuracy of recorded system input activities.

Output:
Detailed report evaluating the compliance of system inputs with set requirements.
Analysis documentation of validation process effectiveness.
Compiled log assessments ensuring accurate and complete record-keeping.

controls.title: Breach Notification Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure timely and appropriate notification of breaches and incidents to affected data subjects, regulators, and other relevant parties.
controls.successCriteria:
All breach notifications comply with the established procedures.
Notifications are timely, complete, and accurately reflect the nature and extent of the breach.
Compliance with legal and regulatory requirements in breach notification.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Collect all records of data breaches and incidents reported in the review period.
Obtain copies of breach notifications issued to affected parties.
Review policy documents outlining the breach notification process.

Analysis:
Verify each incident against the breach notification policy to ensure compliance with notification procedures.
Assess the timeliness and adequacy of notifications sent to affected data subjects and regulators.
Check for completeness and accuracy in the content of the notifications.

Output:
A report summarizing adherence to breach notification procedures.
Analysis of the timeliness and adequacy of breach notifications.
Documentation highlighting any discrepancies or non-compliance in breach notification.

controls.title: URL Request Audit Logging
controls.objective: Ensure the collection and storage of URL request audit logs on all enterprise assets where appropriate and supported, to monitor and analyze web traffic for security and operational efficiency.
controls.successCriteria:
All enterprise assets that support URL request logging have it enabled and properly configured.
URL request audit logs are regularly reviewed and stored in accordance with organizational policies and standards.
Any irregularities or security concerns identified in the URL request logs are addressed and documented.
Including existing criteria, such as all accounts and their roles being as per the Access Matrix, and change requests following the procedure.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve a list of all enterprise assets capable of URL request logging.
Obtain URL request audit logs from a representative sample of these assets.
Acquire documentation on the configuration settings for URL request logging on these assets.
In addition to the existing evidence gathering steps, like getting the list of systems from the Access Matrix, etc.

Analysis:
Verify that all listed enterprise assets have URL request logging enabled and are appropriately configured.
Examine the URL request audit logs for signs of irregular activity or patterns that might indicate security concerns.
Cross-reference the URL request logs with other network activity logs for comprehensive analysis.
Plus the existing analysis steps, such as comparing accounts against the Access Matrix, reviewing change requests, etc.

Output:
A comprehensive list of enterprise assets with their URL request logging status.
Copies of URL request audit logs.
A report detailing the analysis of the URL request logs and any findings or recommendations.
Along with the existing outputs, like screenshots, spreadsheets checking each account, etc.

controls.title: Privileged Access Rights Management
controls.objective: Ensure that the allocation, use, and management of privileged access rights are restricted and controlled in accordance with the organization's access control policy and industry best practices.
controls.successCriteria:
All privileged accounts and their roles are in accordance with the Access Control Matrix.
All change requests for privileged access have followed the established procedure and policy.
The authorization process for privileged access rights is robust and compliant with organizational standards.
Effective measures are in place for the awareness, management, and expiry of privileged access rights.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the list of systems with privileged access rights (from Access Control Matrix).
Capture screenshots of privileged accounts on each system.
Retrieve the list of privileged access change requests and approvals since the last audit.
Gather documentation on the authorization process for privileged access rights.

Analysis:
For each privileged account on the system, verify if it aligns with the Access Control Matrix.
Review privileged access change requests to ensure they have followed the organization's access control policy.
Assess the authorization process and records for granting and revoking privileged access rights.
Examine the management and expiry procedures for privileged access rights.
Evaluate the measures taken to make users aware of their privileged access rights and mode.
Analyze the re-authentication or authentication step-up processes implemented for privileged access rights.

Output:
Screenshots documenting the privileged accounts on each system.
A spreadsheet cross-referencing each privileged account with the Access Control Matrix.
A spreadsheet detailing and verifying each privileged access change request and its compliance with the policy.
A report on the assessment of the authorization process and management of privileged access rights.

controls.title: Warning and Communication Procedures
controls.objective: Ensure effective communication and coordination with internal and external stakeholders during a disruption to minimize impact and facilitate a structured response.
controls.successCriteria:
Communication procedures are comprehensive, detailing all required aspects (what, when, with whom, how).
Emergency contact information is up-to-date and readily available.
All communications during disruptions are logged and documented.
The media response plan is clear and has been followed during incidents.
Structured communication with emergency responders is evidenced.
Records of disruptions show detailed documentation of actions and decisions.
Exercises of communication procedures are conducted regularly, with reports indicating successful outcomes.
Interested parties are appropriately alerted during disruptions.
Coordination between responding organizations is evidenced and effective.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:

Obtain the documented communication procedures outlining the communication process with internal and external interested parties.
Get the list of emergency contact information for employees and key stakeholders.
Acquire records of communications sent and received during the last disruption.
Retrieve the details of the organization’s media response plan.
Collect records of communications with emergency responders.
Gather evidence of periodic exercises of the communication procedures, including after-action reports.

Analysis:

Verify that the communication procedures include details on what, when, with whom, and how to communicate during a disruption.
Ensure that the procedures cover how to receive, document, and respond to communications from interested parties, including national or regional risk advisory systems.
Check for evidence that means of communication were available and used during a disruption.
Review records to confirm that structured communication was facilitated with emergency responders.
Validate that a media response plan exists and was followed, including a communication strategy.
Examine logs of disruptions to confirm that details, actions taken, and decisions made were documented.
Ensure procedures include alerting interested parties potentially impacted by a disruption.
Confirm that coordination and communication with multiple responding organizations were appropriately managed.
Review the exercise programme to verify that the warning and communication procedures are tested regularly.

Output:

Screenshots of documented communication procedures.
List of emergency contact information.
Records and screenshots of communication logs during disruptions.
Copy of the media response plan.
Records of communications with emergency responders.
After-action reports from exercise programmes.

controls.title: Legal Compliance Verification
controls.objective: To ensure continuous alignment with legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to information security.
controls.successCriteria:
All legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements are identified, documented, and regularly updated.
Compliance actions, policies, and training are effectively aligned with these requirements.
Any specific legal advice or consultations are adequately addressing the relevant legal complexities.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the most recent documentation of legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual obligations.
Gather records of compliance efforts, including policy updates, training logs, and compliance reports.
Retrieve documentation of any legal advice or consultations sought, particularly regarding cryptography and cross-border data transfers.

Analysis:
Review the documentation of legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual obligations to ensure it is current and comprehensive.
Examine compliance efforts to verify they are in line with these obligations, focusing on policy implementation, employee training, and reporting mechanisms.
Assess the adequacy of legal consultations, especially in areas with specific legal requirements like cryptography.

Output:
A report detailing the status of compliance with each legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirement.
A compliance matrix aligning obligations with specific actions and policies.
A summary of legal consultations and advice, highlighting any areas of concern.

controls.title: Business Continuity Strategy Review
controls.objective: Ensure the effectiveness and adequacy of business continuity strategies and solutions based on the outputs from business impact analysis (BIA) and risk assessment.
controls.successCriteria:
All identified strategies meet the BIA and Risk Assessment requirements.
Strategies demonstrate a comprehensive approach to managing disruptions before, during, and after events.
Adequate resources are allocated and available for implementing the strategies.
Past disruptions are effectively mitigated, and recovery objectives are met within the required timeframes.
The organization demonstrates continuous improvement in its business continuity planning.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:

Obtain the latest Business Impact Analysis (BIA) report.
Obtain the latest Risk Assessment report.
Collect the documented business continuity strategies and solutions.
Gather records of disruptions and the implemented continuity strategies from the past year.

Analysis:

Strategy Review:
Compare the identified strategies in the documentation with the requirements stated in the BIA and Risk Assessment.
Verify that the strategies meet the requirements for continuing and recovering prioritized activities within identified timeframes and agreed capacity.
Ensure the strategies consider options for before, during, and after disruptions.
Confirm that the strategies include measures to reduce the likelihood of disruptions, shorten the period of disruption, and limit the impact on the organization's products and services.
Check that the strategies account for the availability of adequate resources (people, data, infrastructure, ICT, etc.).

Implementation Review:
Validate that the selected strategies align with the organization's risk appetite and consider associated costs and benefits.
Verify that resources required for implementing the strategies are adequately determined and available.
Review records of disruptions to assess whether the implemented strategies effectively mitigated the impact and met recovery objectives.

Output:

Documentation comparing strategies with BIA and Risk Assessment requirements.
List of identified gaps and recommendations for improvement.
Report on the adequacy of resources allocated for the strategies.
Record of analysis of past disruptions and effectiveness of the implemented strategies.
Spreadsheet summarizing the findings and compliance status of each strategy.

controls.title: Sector-Specific Risk Tolerance Analysis
controls.objective: To ensure that the organization's determination of risk tolerance is informed and aligned with its role in critical infrastructure and influenced by sector-specific risk analysis.
controls.successCriteria:
The Risk Management Policy and Risk Register accurately reflect the organization's risk tolerance as influenced by its role in critical infrastructure and sector-specific risk analysis.
Evidence shows that sector-specific risk analyses are actively used to inform and update the organization's risk tolerance levels.
Decision-making processes regarding risk tolerance are documented and demonstrate consideration of sector-specific risks and critical infrastructure role.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Collect the organization's Risk Management Policy and Risk Register.
Gather documentation on sector-specific risk analyses and reports.
Obtain records of decision-making processes regarding risk tolerance levels.

Analysis:
Review the Risk Management Policy and Risk Register to identify how risk tolerance levels are defined and updated.
Examine sector-specific risk analyses to determine how they inform the organization's risk tolerance.
Analyze decision-making records to confirm that sector-specific information and critical infrastructure considerations are incorporated into risk tolerance determinations.

Output:
A report summarizing findings from the Risk Management Policy and Risk Register review.
Compilation of sector-specific risk analyses and how they align with the organization's risk tolerance.
Analysis of decision-making records regarding risk tolerance.

controls.title: Capacity Management Oversight
controls.objective: To ensure effective monitoring and adjustment of resource usage in line with current and projected capacity requirements for information processing facilities, human resources, and other critical systems.
controls.successCriteria:
The Capacity Management Plan accurately reflects current and projected business needs and IT infrastructure.
System utilization is optimized and aligned with the organization’s capacity requirements.
Historical trends and future projections of resource usage are realistic and actively managed.
Stress-tests confirm system readiness for peak demands.
Management demonstrates proactive engagement and decision-making in capacity management matters.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the latest Capacity Management Plan and related documents.
Gather system utilization reports for key information processing facilities, including cloud services if applicable.
Collect historical data on resource usage trends and future projections.
Retrieve records of recent stress-tests conducted on systems and services.
Acquire minutes of meetings where capacity management issues and decisions were discussed.

Analysis:
Review the Capacity Management Plan for completeness and alignment with current business needs and IT infrastructure.
Analyze system utilization reports to verify that system tuning and monitoring are being effectively applied.
Compare historical and projected resource usage trends to assess the accuracy of future capacity projections.
Evaluate the outcomes of stress-tests to confirm system readiness for peak performance requirements.
Scrutinize meeting minutes to ensure ongoing managerial engagement and proactive decision-making regarding capacity management.

Output:
A compiled report detailing findings from the Capacity Management Plan and utilization reports.
A comparative analysis of historical vs. projected resource usage.
Summary of stress-test results and their implications.
Overview of management discussions and decisions on capacity management.

controls.title: Equipment Security and Protection
controls.objective: Ensure secure siting and protection of equipment to minimize risks from physical and environmental threats, unauthorized access, and information leakage.
controls.successCriteria:
All equipment is sited securely and in compliance with organizational guidelines for minimizing physical and environmental risks.
Effective controls are in place and functioning as intended to protect against physical and environmental threats.
Only authorized personnel have access to sensitive equipment areas.
Environmental conditions are consistently monitored and maintained within safe parameters for equipment operation.
Compliance with policies and procedures for handling sensitive data and protecting equipment from information leakage and other risks.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Compile a comprehensive inventory of all information processing equipment.
Obtain floor plans showing the current siting of all equipment.
Collect policies and procedures related to equipment protection, including guidelines for environmental conditions, physical security measures, and handling of sensitive data.
Gather records of environmental monitoring (temperature, humidity, etc.) for areas with critical equipment.
Acquire documentation on physical and environmental protection measures implemented (e.g., lightning protection, vibration control, electromagnetic shielding).
Retrieve logs or records of access to areas with sensitive equipment.

Analysis:
Cross-reference equipment inventory with floor plans to verify secure and appropriate siting.
Evaluate existing protection measures against best practices for physical and environmental security.
Review environmental monitoring records to identify any conditions potentially harmful to equipment operation.
Assess access logs to ensure only authorized personnel have access to sensitive equipment areas.
Examine compliance with established guidelines for handling sensitive data and equipment, including checking against risks like water damage, fire, theft, and electromagnetic interference.

Output:
A report detailing the findings from the cross-referencing of equipment siting.
An assessment report on the adequacy of physical and environmental protection measures.
A summary of environmental condition monitoring results and any identified risks.
An analysis of access control effectiveness.
Compliance report regarding data handling and equipment protection guidelines.

controls.title: Confidentiality Agreement Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure that all employees, contractors, and third parties have signed confidentiality agreements that are compliant with the organization's requirements for the protection of information.
controls.successCriteria:
Every individual on the list has a signed agreement that is current and compliant with the organization's standards.
All agreements reflect the latest template in terms of clauses and legal enforceability.
Evidence of regular review and updating of the confidentiality agreement template.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a complete list of current employees, contractors, and third parties engaged with the organization.
Collect copies of signed confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements for each individual on the list.
Gather records of the most recent review and update of the confidentiality agreement templates.

Analysis:
Verify that each individual on the employee, contractor, and third-party list has a corresponding signed confidentiality agreement.
Compare the terms of each signed agreement against the current template to ensure they include all necessary clauses and meet the organization's current standards.
Assess the dates of agreement signings and template reviews to ensure periodic review and updating are being followed.

Output:
A spreadsheet listing all individuals with a column indicating whether they have a corresponding signed agreement.
A report summarizing any discrepancies between individual agreements and the current template.
Documentation of the last review and update of the confidentiality agreement template.

controls.title: Information Deletion Verification
controls.objective: Ensure the secure and compliant deletion of sensitive information from systems, devices, and storage media when no longer required.
controls.successCriteria:
All sensitive information is deleted in accordance with the organization's data retention policy and relevant laws.
Proper documentation and evidence of deletion are maintained for all deletion activities.
Third-party service providers (if used) comply with the organization's requirements for secure information deletion.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of systems, devices, and storage media where sensitive information is stored (referencing the Data Retention and Deletion Policy).
Gather documentation or logs of deletion activities, including methods used (e.g., electronic overwriting, cryptographic erasure).
Collect evidence of deletion verification from third-party service providers, if applicable.

Analysis:
Verify that the deletion methods used align with the organization's data retention policy and relevant laws and regulations.
Confirm that each deletion activity is properly documented and that evidence of deletion is recorded.
For third-party service providers, ensure that the evidence of deletion provided by them is valid and complies with the organization's requirements.

Output:
A report detailing the deletion methods used and their compliance with policies and regulations.
A log or spreadsheet verifying each deletion activity and its adherence to the policy.
A summary of third-party compliance with the information deletion requirement.

controls.title: Inquiry and Complaints Resolution
controls.objective: To ensure effective receipt, addressing, and resolution of inquiries, complaints, and disputes from data subjects and others.
controls.successCriteria:
All inquiries, complaints, and disputes are handled in a timely, effective, and compliant manner as per established procedures.
Adequate documentation and communication are maintained for each case.
Continuous monitoring and improvement are evident in handling inquiries and complaints.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Collect records of all inquiries, complaints, and disputes received since the last audit.
Obtain documentation of the responses and resolutions provided.
Review policy documents and procedures related to the management of inquiries, complaints, and disputes.

Analysis:
Assess each case to verify adherence to the established resolution process.
Evaluate the timeliness and effectiveness of responses and resolutions.
Check for completeness and appropriateness of communication with the individuals who raised the inquiries or complaints.

Output:
A comprehensive report detailing the handling of inquiries, complaints, and disputes.
Analysis summary of resolution effectiveness and timeliness.
Documentation of any gaps or non-compliance in the resolution process.

controls.title: Threat Identification and Documentation
controls.objective: To ensure that all internal and external threats are identified, documented, and maintained in the Risk Register in accordance with organizational cybersecurity policies.
controls.successCriteria:
The Risk Register accurately reflects all identified internal and external threats.
The process for identifying and documenting threats is consistently followed and documented.
Risk assessment activities align with the entries in the Risk Register, ensuring thoroughness and accuracy.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the most recent version of the Risk Register.
Collect documentation of the process for identifying and documenting threats (e.g., meeting minutes, emails, reports).
Retrieve the records of the latest risk assessment activities.

Analysis:
Review the Risk Register to ensure it includes both internal and external threats, clearly identifying each threat's nature, source, and potential impact.
Verify that the process for identifying and documenting threats is followed as per the organization’s policy, including the assessment of new threats and updates to existing threats.
Compare the Risk Register entries with the records of risk assessment activities to confirm consistency and completeness.

Output:
Annotated Risk Register highlighting reviewed entries.
Report summarizing compliance with the threat identification and documentation process.
Comparative analysis of Risk Register and risk assessment records.

controls.title: Dormant Account Management
controls.objective: Ensure all dormant accounts are identified and either disabled or deleted after 45 days of inactivity.
controls.successCriteria:
All user accounts are active or have been disabled/deleted as per the 45-day inactivity policy.
Documentation and system logs corroborate the actions taken on dormant accounts.
No accounts remain active beyond 45 days of inactivity without appropriate justification or exception.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all user accounts from the system's user management database.
Retrieve system logs or reports showing the last login date for each account.
Access records of disabled or deleted accounts for the period since the last audit.

Analysis:
For each account, calculate the duration of inactivity since the last login date.
