controls.objective: To ensure that read and write access to source code, development tools, and software libraries are appropriately managed and restricted based on personnel roles and business needs, in accordance with the organization's security policies.
controls.successCriteria:
All instances of access to source code, development tools, and software libraries align with the permissions defined in the Access Control Matrix.
All change requests related to source code access have been executed following established procedures and with appropriate authorizations.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the Access Control Matrix specifying access rights for source code repositories and development tools.
Collect system logs showing read and write access to source code, development tools, and software libraries.
Gather documentation of change requests related to source code access since the last audit.

Analysis:
Compare the actual access rights (from system logs) against the Access Control Matrix to verify compliance.
Review change request documentation to ensure each request follows established change control procedures and proper authorization was obtained.
Examine system logs for unauthorized or anomalous access patterns, focusing on write access to source code.

Output:
A report detailing compliance with the Access Control Matrix.
A spreadsheet or database cross-referencing each access instance against the Access Control Matrix.
Compiled documentation and analysis of change requests.

controls.title: Access and Identity Security Management
controls.objective: To ensure secure and compliant management of access control, identity lifecycle, authentication information, and access rights in alignment with organizational policies and security requirements.
controls.successCriteria:
All aspects of access control, identity management, authentication information, and access rights are managed securely, efficiently, and in full compliance with organizational policies.
Identities, both human and non-human, are managed with accountability and transparency.
Authentication information is allocated, managed, and protected securely, with user compliance to guidelines.
Access rights are provisioned, reviewed, and modified in accordance with organizational needs and security requirements, with special attention to changes in roles or employment status.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain policy documents for Access Control, Identity Management, Authentication Information Management, and Access Rights.
Collect records of identity assignments, shared identity approvals, access logs, change requests, and identity disablement or removal actions.
Gather logs and records related to authentication information allocation, transmission, receipt, and changes.
Acquire documentation and records for access rights provisioning, review, modification, and revocation.

Analysis:
Review policy adherence in managing access control, identity lifecycle, authentication information, and access rights.
Verify the uniqueness and appropriateness of identity assignments and management, including shared identities and non-human entities.
Assess the security and compliance in allocation, transmission, and management of authentication information.
Analyze access rights management processes, including provisioning, modification, revocation, and regular reviews, especially after changes in roles or employment status.

Output:
A comprehensive report detailing compliance and adherence to policies across all areas.
Summaries of identity management, authentication information practices, and access rights governance.
Analysis of logs and records for irregularities or non-compliance.

controls.title: Trusted DNS Server Configuration
controls.objective: Ensure all enterprise assets are configured to use designated, trusted DNS servers to enhance network security and integrity.
controls.successCriteria:
All audited assets use the designated trusted DNS servers as per the enterprise standards.
Any deviations from the standard DNS configurations are properly justified and documented.
DNS configuration changes have followed the established change management process.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Retrieve a comprehensive list of all enterprise assets.
Collect network configuration details from a representative sample of assets, focusing on DNS server settings.
Gather documentation or configurations that specify the enterprise’s trusted DNS servers (either internal or reputable external servers).

Analysis:
For each asset in the sample, verify that the DNS server settings align with the enterprise’s designated trusted DNS servers.
Assess any deviations to determine if they are justified or require remediation.
Review change management records to confirm that any updates to DNS settings were properly authorized and documented.

Output:
A report detailing DNS configurations for each sampled asset.
A compliance matrix comparing current DNS settings against the enterprise standard.
A summary of change management records related to DNS configuration changes.

controls.title: Outsourced Development Oversight
controls.objective: To ensure that outsourced system development activities comply with the organization's information security requirements and expectations.
controls.successCriteria:
All outsourced development projects have contracts or agreements that meet the organization's security requirements.
Acceptance testing and assurance reports demonstrate compliance with security and privacy standards.
Intellectual property rights, threat models, and escrow agreements are properly managed and documented.
Supplier development processes have been audited and found compliant with the organization's standards.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all outsourced development projects and associated suppliers.
Acquire copies of signed contracts or agreements with these suppliers, highlighting security and development requirements.
Collect recent acceptance testing reports, security and privacy assurance reports, and any documentation related to code ownership and intellectual property rights.
Gather evidence of threat models provided to the suppliers and any escrow agreements in place.
Obtain logs or reports of any audits conducted on the supplier's development processes.

Analysis:
Verify that each outsourced project has a corresponding contract or agreement that includes the necessary security and development requirements.
Assess the acceptance testing reports and other assurance reports to ensure they meet the minimum acceptable levels of security and privacy.
Confirm that threat models, intellectual property rights, and escrow agreements are appropriately addressed and documented.
Review the audit logs or reports to validate that the supplier's development processes comply with the contractual requirements.

Output:
A comprehensive report detailing the compliance status of each outsourced development project.
A checklist verifying the presence and adequacy of contracts, testing reports, intellectual property documentation, threat models, and audit logs.

controls.title: Endpoint Device Security Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that all user endpoint devices adhere to the organization's IT security policies and standards for handling and protecting information.
controls.successCriteria:
All user endpoint devices in the sample are registered and comply with physical protection, software, encryption, and security update requirements as per policy.
Users have acknowledged and adhere to the endpoint device security policy.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain an inventory list of all user endpoint devices (laptops, mobile phones, etc.) from the IT department.
Gather configuration reports and security settings for a sample of endpoint devices.
Collect recent logs of security updates, patch management, and antivirus updates for these devices.
Request records of user acknowledgments or compliance attestations regarding the endpoint device security policy.

Analysis:
Cross-check the inventory list against the policy's requirements for device registration, physical protection, software restrictions, encryption, and other specified security measures.
Verify that the security settings on sampled devices comply with the policy (e.g., firewalls, automatic updates, access controls).
Assess the currency of security updates and patch management against policy requirements.
Evaluate user acknowledgments and compliance attestations to ensure understanding and adherence to the policy.

Output:
A compiled inventory and configuration report for the sampled devices.
A compliance matrix showing each device's adherence to the policy's requirements.
A summary of security updates and patch management status for these devices.
Documentation of user acknowledgments or compliance attestations.

controls.title: Supplier Security Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that suppliers adhere to the organization's information security standards and requirements throughout the duration of their engagement.
controls.successCriteria:
All suppliers have clearly documented security requirements in their contracts/agreements.
Suppliers demonstrate compliance with the organization's security standards.
Effective monitoring and management of supplier-related security incidents.
Prompt and appropriate action taken in response to non-compliance or security issues.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the list of current suppliers from the Supplier Management Register.
Gather copies of security clauses or requirements from contracts/agreements with these suppliers.
Collect recent audit reports, security assessments, or compliance certificates from suppliers.
Retrieve incident logs or non-compliance reports related to suppliers.
Gather documentation of supplier monitoring activities, such as regular review meetings or communication records.

Analysis:
Review each supplier’s contract/agreement to confirm the inclusion and clarity of information security requirements.
Cross-examine audit reports, assessments, or certificates against the organization's security standards to verify compliance.
Analyze incident logs for patterns or repeated security issues with specific suppliers.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the monitoring process - frequency, depth, and responsiveness to issues.

Output:
Compiled list of suppliers with associated security requirements from contracts.
Comparison spreadsheet of supplier compliance vs. organization's security standards.
Summary report of incidents and non-compliance issues.
Evaluation report on the supplier monitoring process.

controls.title: Information Security Continuity Control
controls.objective: To ensure the maintenance of information security at an appropriate level during disruptions and integrate security controls in business continuity processes.
controls.successCriteria:
The BCP and ICT Continuity Plans adequately address information security requirements during disruptions.
The implemented security controls and compensating controls are effective and comply with industry best practices.
Response actions taken during disruptions align with the planned procedures and effectively maintain information security.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the Business Continuity Plan (BCP) and ICT Continuity Plan documents.
Get records of the latest tests and reviews of the BCP.
Acquire a list of information security controls and compensating controls implemented for disruptions.
Obtain logs or records of any recent disruptions and the response actions taken.

Analysis:
Review the BCP and ICT Continuity Plan to ensure they include specific information security requirements and controls for disruptions.
Examine the test and review records of the BCP to check for the effectiveness and relevance of the plans in maintaining information security during disruptions.
Evaluate the list of implemented security controls and compensating controls against industry best practices and specific organizational needs during disruptions.
Analyze the response actions taken during recent disruptions to assess the effectiveness of current security measures and identify any potential gaps.

Output:
A detailed report on the evaluation of the BCP and ICT Continuity Plans.
A compliance matrix comparing current controls with industry best practices.
A gap analysis report from recent disruption responses.

controls.title: POS SSL/TLS Security Assurance
controls.objective: Ensure all POS POI terminals are not susceptible to any known exploits for SSL/early TLS and maintain verified documentation to prove compliance.
controls.successCriteria:
All POS POI terminals must have up-to-date and complete documentation verifying their security against known exploits for SSL/early TLS.
Patch and update logs must show that terminals were updated promptly in response to new vulnerabilities.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all POS POI terminals in use from the Asset Inventory.
Collect documentation proving each terminal's compliance with SSL/early TLS security, including vendor attestations, system/network configuration details.
Gather a list of all security patches and updates applied to the POS POI terminals since the last audit.

Analysis:
Cross-reference each POS POI terminal against the latest known vulnerabilities for SSL/early TLS to confirm there is no susceptibility.
Verify the documentation for its completeness and relevance to each POS POI terminal.
Review the patch and update history to ensure immediate action has been taken for any new vulnerabilities discovered since the last audit.

Output:
A report detailing each POS POI terminal and its compliance status with SSL/early TLS security requirements.
Documentation verification logs for each POS POI terminal.
A history log of patches and updates applied to POS POI terminals.

controls.title: Application Security Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure that all applications, whether developed in-house or acquired, meet established information security requirements.
controls.successCriteria:
All applications have documented security requirements aligned with the organization's application security policy.
Security testing and code reviews have been conducted for all applications, with documented resolution of identified vulnerabilities.
No critical security vulnerabilities remain unaddressed in the reviewed applications.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain a list of all applications developed or acquired during the audit period.
Gather documentation of security requirements for each application, including risk assessments, design documents, and security testing reports.
Collect records of security code reviews and penetration test results for each application.

Analysis:
For each application, verify that security requirements were identified and documented during the development or acquisition phase.
Assess the completeness and adequacy of the security requirements against the organization’s application security policy.
Review security testing reports and code review records to ensure that all identified vulnerabilities were addressed and mitigated.
Compare penetration test results with initial security requirements to ensure that applications meet the desired security standards.

Output:
A report detailing the security requirements documentation for each application.
A comparison matrix of identified vs. implemented security measures for each application.
A summary of security testing outcomes and any outstanding vulnerabilities or issues.

controls.title: Data Specification Management
controls.objective: Ensure the identification and definition of data specifications required to support the use of products and services.
controls.successCriteria:
All data specifications are accurately defined, including detailed descriptions of population, elements, sources, and measurements.
Metadata accurately and completely describes each data element and the data population.
Effective communication of data definitions and specifications to all relevant stakeholders.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the documentation outlining data specifications for all key products and services.
Collect metadata descriptions and usage guidelines for the data sets used.
Gather records showing communication of data definitions and purposes to relevant stakeholders.

Analysis:
Review the documentation to verify that it includes comprehensive data specifications (e.g., population, nature of elements, sources, units of measurement).
Assess the metadata for completeness and accuracy.
Evaluate communication records to ensure proper dissemination of data specifications and definitions.

Output:
Compiled documentation of data specifications.
Analysis report on metadata completeness and accuracy.
Log of communications pertaining to data definitions and specifications.

controls.title: Employment Agreement Compliance
controls.objective: Ensure all employees, contractors, and third parties adhere to the information security responsibilities and legal obligations outlined in their employment contractual agreements.
controls.successCriteria:
All employment contracts and confidentiality agreements include comprehensive information security responsibilities and legal obligations.
Actual practices of handling information security and legal responsibilities are in full compliance with the terms stated in the agreements.
Disciplinary actions for non-compliance are consistently applied in accordance with the terms of employment.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain copies of employment contracts, confidentiality agreements, and other relevant documents for all employees, contractors, and third parties.
Gather documentation detailing information classification responsibilities, legal rights and responsibilities, and procedures for handling information from interested parties.
Collect records of disciplinary actions taken for non-compliance with information security requirements.

Analysis:
Review employment contracts and confidentiality agreements to verify the inclusion of information security responsibilities and legal obligations.
Compare the documented responsibilities with actual practices to assess adherence.
Examine records of disciplinary actions to ensure they align with the terms stated in the employment agreements.

Output:
A report summarizing the review of employment contracts and confidentiality agreements.
A compliance matrix comparing stated responsibilities in agreements with actual practices.
A summary of disciplinary actions taken, correlated with the respective breaches of employment terms.

controls.title: BCMS Awareness and Training
controls.objective: Ensure that all personnel are aware of the business continuity policy, their roles and responsibilities, and the importance of adhering to BCMS requirements.
controls.successCriteria:
All employees have completed mandatory BCMS training.
Training materials comprehensively cover required topics.
Regular and clear communication of BCMS information.
100% of employees have signed acknowledgements.
High pass rate on assessments/quizzes indicating sufficient understanding of BCMS.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:

Training Records:

Obtain a list of all employees and contractors.
Get copies of attendance records for BCMS training sessions.
Obtain training materials (slides, handouts, etc.) used in the sessions.

Communication Logs:

Retrieve records of communications sent regarding BCMS policy and procedures (emails, memos, intranet posts).
Get screenshots or copies of announcements related to business continuity.

Employee Acknowledgements:

Obtain signed acknowledgements from employees confirming their understanding of the BCMS policy and their roles.
Collect records of any assessments or quizzes conducted to test understanding of BCMS topics.

Analysis:

Training Participation:

Verify that all employees have participated in mandatory BCMS training sessions.
Ensure that training materials cover all required topics (business continuity policy, roles and responsibilities, implications of non-conformance).

Communication Effectiveness:

Review communication logs to confirm that information about BCMS is regularly and effectively disseminated.
Ensure that key messages about roles and responsibilities before, during, and after disruptions are clearly communicated.

Acknowledgements and Understanding:

Check that all employees have signed acknowledgements.
Review assessment results to ensure employees have a sufficient understanding of the BCMS.

Output:

Attendance records and training materials.
Communication logs and copies of announcements.
Signed employee acknowledgements and assessment results.
A report summarizing compliance with the awareness requirements and any identified gaps.

controls.title: BCMS Documentation and Capability Evaluation
controls.objective: Ensure the continuous evaluation and improvement of business continuity documentation and capabilities to maintain organizational resilience and compliance with legal, regulatory, and industry standards.
controls.successCriteria:
All BIA, RA, strategies, solutions, plans, and procedures are current and have undergone regular reviews as per the planned intervals.
Evaluations comprehensively cover all required aspects, including partners' and suppliers' capabilities.
Compliance with all applicable legal and regulatory requirements, industry best practices, and internal business continuity policies and objectives is demonstrated.
Documentation and procedures are promptly updated following evaluations, incidents, activations, and significant changes.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:

Obtain the latest versions of the Business Impact Analysis (BIA), Risk Assessment (RA), strategies, solutions, plans, and procedures.
Collect records of evaluations conducted, including reviews, analysis reports, exercise results, test outcomes, post-incident reports, and performance evaluations.
Retrieve documentation of evaluations conducted on the business continuity capabilities of relevant partners and suppliers.
Gather evidence of compliance checks against legal and regulatory requirements, industry best practices, and internal business continuity policies and objectives.
Collect records of updates made to documentation and procedures following evaluations, incidents, activations, and significant changes.

Analysis:

Verify that the BIA, RA, strategies, solutions, plans, and procedures are up-to-date and have been reviewed at the planned intervals.
Analyze evaluation records to ensure they cover reviews, analyses, exercises, tests, post-incident reports, and performance evaluations.
Check that evaluations include assessments of partners' and suppliers' business continuity capabilities.
Compare compliance documentation against legal and regulatory requirements, industry best practices, and internal policies and objectives to ensure adherence.
Confirm that documentation and procedures have been updated in a timely manner following evaluations, incidents, activations, and significant changes.

Output:

Compilation of the latest versions of BIA, RA, strategies, solutions, plans, and procedures.
Detailed report summarizing the findings of reviews, analyses, exercises, tests, post-incident reports, and performance evaluations.
Evaluation summary of partners' and suppliers' business continuity capabilities.
Compliance checklist against legal and regulatory requirements, industry best practices, and internal policies and objectives.
Log of updates made to documentation and procedures.

controls.title: Information Backup Verification
controls.objective: To ensure that backups of information, software, and systems are conducted accurately and regularly, in alignment with the organization's backup policy.
controls.successCriteria:
All backups are conducted in accordance with the set schedule and backup policy.
Backup logs confirm the completion of scheduled backups without significant failures.
Backup testing exercises demonstrate the reliability and effectiveness of the backups in data restoration scenarios.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the latest backup schedule documentation.
Retrieve logs of recent backup activities.
Gather a list of critical systems and data requiring backup as per the backup policy.
Secure screenshots or reports demonstrating successful backup completions.
Acquire documentation of the last few backup testing exercises, including dates and outcomes.

Analysis:
Review the backup schedule against the backup policy to confirm adherence to prescribed frequency and types (full, incremental, differential).
For each logged backup activity, verify that it corresponds with the scheduled backups and includes all critical systems and data.
Assess the results of backup testing exercises to confirm the reliability and effectiveness of the backups in various scenarios, including potential data restoration.

Output:
A comprehensive report summarizing the findings from the backup schedule, logs, and testing exercises.
A spreadsheet or checklist comparing scheduled backups with actual backup logs.
A summary of backup testing exercises, indicating successes and areas for improvement.

controls.title: Remote Work Security Compliance
controls.objective: To ensure that all aspects of remote working adhere to the organization’s security standards and policies.
controls.successCriteria:
All remote workers comply with the organization’s remote working policies and guidelines.
Remote devices are configured and secured as per the IT Security Policy standards.
Remote workers have completed necessary training and are aware of secure working practices.
Adequate physical security measures are in place at remote work locations.
Effective backup and emergency procedures are established and followed in remote working environments.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Collect remote working policies and guidelines issued by the organization.
Obtain a list of all employees engaged in remote work.
Gather records of remote access logs and usage data.
Acquire security configuration details of remote devices (laptops, mobile phones, etc.).
Retrieve documentation on training and awareness programs conducted for remote working.

Analysis:
Cross-reference the list of remote workers with the remote access logs to verify authorized access.
Review security configurations of remote devices against the IT Security Policy standards.
Assess whether remote workers have completed the required training for secure remote working.
Evaluate the physical security measures implemented at remote work locations.
Inspect procedures and practices for data backup and emergency responses in remote working settings.

Output:
A report summarizing the compliance status of each remote worker.
A spreadsheet detailing the security configuration of each remote device.
Documentation on training completion and awareness levels among remote workers.
An assessment of physical security measures at remote locations.
An analysis of backup and emergency procedures in place for remote work.

controls.title: Asset Inventory Management
controls.objective: Ensure a comprehensive and up-to-date inventory of all organizational assets, including detailed information on network and hardware addresses, machine names, data asset owners, departments, and network connection approvals.
controls.successCriteria:
The asset inventory is complete, accurate, and includes all required details such as network and hardware addresses, machine names, data asset owners, departments, and network approval status.
Ownership is correctly assigned and documented for all listed assets, with additional details accurately recorded.
Asset return forms are complete and comply with policy requirements.
Asset tracking logs confirm timely and proper return of assets, with all additional asset details appropriately documented.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the current Inventory of Information and Associated Assets document.
Gather ownership assignment records for each listed asset and signed asset return forms for terminated or changed employment cases.
Acquire logs of recent updates or changes made to the asset inventory and records of asset tracking upon employee exit.

Analysis:
Review the inventory document for completeness and accuracy, ensuring all assets are listed with appropriate details.
Cross-check ownership records against each asset and returned assets against the Asset Registry.
Analyze change logs and asset tracking logs to verify that inventory updates and asset returns are consistent, timely, and comply with policy.

Output:
A comprehensive report detailing findings from the inventory and asset return review.
A spreadsheet or database capturing the alignment of ownership records with the asset inventory and returned assets.
Summary of discrepancies or gaps identified during the analysis.

controls.title: Control Activities Implementation and Management
controls.objective: Ensure that control activities are effectively integrated into business processes, executed responsibly and timely, and periodically reassessed for relevance and effectiveness.
controls.successCriteria:
All control activities are clearly defined and aligned with business processes.
Clear responsibility and accountability for executing control activities are established and adhered to.
Control activities are performed within the specified timelines.
Corrective actions are appropriately and timely taken in response to issues identified.
Periodic reassessment and updating of control activities are conducted to ensure their continued relevance and effectiveness.
controls.testingMethodology:
Evidence:
Obtain the documentation of established policies and procedures that detail control activities across various business units or functions.
Collect records of responsibility assignments for executing these policies and procedures.
Gather evidence of timely performance of control activities, such as logs, reports, or compliance tracking tools.
Secure documentation of any corrective actions taken in response to issues identified through control activities.
Access records of the latest reviews and updates made to the control activities.

Analysis:
Review the policies and procedures to verify they clearly define control activities and align with business processes.
Analyze responsibility assignment records to ensure clear accountability for the execution of control activities.
Examine evidence of control activities' performance to assess if they are conducted within defined timelines.
Evaluate corrective action documents to check for appropriate and timely responses to issues identified.
Assess the records of reviews and updates to control activities for evidence of periodic reassessment and relevance.

Output:
A report outlining the compliance of each business unit or function with the established control activities.
A spreadsheet or document detailing the responsibility assignments for control activities.
A timeline analysis showing the timeliness of control activities execution and corrective actions taken.
A summary report of the most recent reassessment and updating of control activities.
